If Kara Organization launched an immediate attack on Konoha to recover Kawaki before his integration, then what alliances and losses reshape the narrative arc?
If kawaki.relationship_with_naruto never became a paternal bond, then would the subsequent destruction of Konoha by Kawaki be motivated by a completely different logic?

narrative_invariants: Sasuke completes the first part of his revenge against the responsible for the Uchiha clan massacre by killing Danzo, but this murder does not bring him peace or resolution.

Danzo refuses to surrender until he destroys what he possesses, including the Uchiha Shisui eyes he tears out before dying, revealing his obsessive character and vision of a Konoha under his sole rule.
The battle exposes the Sharingan as a political tool of power twisted by Konoha's leaders at the expense of the Uchiha clan, reinforcing Sasuke's belief that the village system is corrupt.
Obito's passive involvement in the battle confirms his role as a manipulator guiding Sasuke toward his own ends without direct exposure.
Sasuke's status as an international criminal becomes official after this event, closing the possibility of a peaceful return to Konoha.
alternative_seeds: If shimura_danzo still had remaining uses of Izanagi available at the moment of the fatal blow, would he have been able to kill Sasuke and alter the power dynamic with Obito?
If uchiha_obito had not logistically supported Sasuke to reach Danzo at the Five Kage Summit, would Sasuke have been able to locate and face Danzo alone?
If shimura_danzo had chosen to publicly reveal his version of the Uchiha clan massacre before dying, would this have affected Konoha's image and Sasuke's trajectory?
If uchiha_sasuke had learned of the Uchiha Shisui eyes in Danzo's arm before the battle and had sought to recover them rather than kill Danzo, how would the battle and its outcomes have evolved?
If one of the other Kage had intervened directly in the battle to protect Danzo or capture Sasuke, would the duel's outcome and political implications have changed?

narrative_invariants: The outcast rejected by the village becomes its supreme leader, closing the narrative arc of social redemption.
Naruto's childhood promise to himself is fulfilled, giving the entire story a archetypal coherence.
The Kakashi-Naruto succession symbolizes the transition from the generation of masters to that of disciples.
Naruto embodies the two ideals he has pursued: being recognized by all and protecting his loved ones from the position of Hokage.
Naruto's ascension to Hokage validates retrospectively every trial he has endured as a necessary step in his formation.
alternative_seeds: If hatake_kakashi.intends_to_retire=false and refuses to step down, would the succession be contested or would Naruto wait indefinitely?
If uzumaki_naruto.jinchuriki_status was still active at the time of the inauguration, would the other nations have accepted a bijuu host as leader of Konoha?
If uzumaki_naruto had died during the Fourth Ninja War, which character would have assumed the role of Seventh Hokage and with what political consequences?
If the village had never recognized Naruto as a war hero, would his ascension to Hokage have caused an internal split in Konoha?
If uzumaki_naruto.married=false at the time of the inauguration, would his emotional isolation have influenced his political decisions as Hokage?

hashirama_madara_meet.name_en: Meeting of Hashirama and Madara as Children
narrative_invariants: The meeting between Hashirama and Madara establishes the emotional foundation for the creation of Konoha, their childhood friendship proving that Senju and Uchiha can coexist.
The two children share a common ideal of peace without knowing it, making their future antagonism all the more tragic.
Their bond is formed outside of any conscious clan identity, making it a genuinely free friendship devoid of inherited hatred.
The river where they meet symbolizes a neutral border, a space outside the perpetual conflict of their era.
This event establishes that clan hatred is culturally constructed and not innate, since two enemies become friends naturally.
alternative_seeds: If senju_hashirama.clan_identity_disclosed=true from the beginning of the meeting, then?
If uchiha_madara.dream_of_peace=false at that age, would the relationship have taken a different form?
If a parent or clan member had witnessed this meeting and interrupted it violently, then?
If Madara had already activated the Sharingan before this meeting and perceived Hashirama as a threat, then?
If a third child from another clan had been present during this meeting, then?

narrative_invariants: The summit marks the first political unity of the five great ninja nations against a common threat, breaking centuries of rivalry and mutual distrust.
Sasuke now embodies a recognized threat by all ninja powers, his name associated with chaos and destruction beyond Konoha.
Danzo is exposed as an illegitimate leader and fleeing before the international community, definitively ending his claim to the Hokage title.
The declaration of war against Akatsuki transforms the conflict from isolated incidents into an open world war, engaging the lives of a whole generation of ninjas.
Gaara's failure to reason with Sasuke confirms that the path of vengeance chosen by Sasuke is now irreversible without major external intervention.
alternative_seeds: If shimura_danzo.sharingan_arm=false and thus without the possibility of using kotoamatsukami, would the summit dynamics have changed to prevent his escape or alter the outcome of his battle with Sasuke?
If uzumaki_naruto.knowledge_of_summit=true and he had been able to intervene in real time, would he have convinced Sasuke or only hastened a direct confrontation between the two former friends?
If gaara.attempt_to_reason_sasuke=true had succeeded, would Sasuke have abandoned his quest against Danzo and what consequences would that have had on the formation of the Ninja Alliance?
If raikage_a had killed uchiha_sasuke during their battle at the summit, would Akatsuki have lost a major asset, causing their war plans to be compromised?
If the five Kage had been unable to agree due to Sasuke's presence and the chaos caused, would the Ninja Alliance have formed through another diplomatic channel before the war began?

narrative_invariants: Obito, by becoming the Juubi Jinchuuriki, concentrates in him a power capable of threatening the entire world and embodies the partial realization of the Moon's Eye Plan.
The transfer of the Juubi to Obito shows that Madara uses him as a disposable instrument for a larger plan.
Becoming the Juubi Jinchuuriki places Obito in a state where his own wills and those of the beast are in tension, weakening his identity.
This event marks the peak of the power held by Obito and the moment when the ninja war reaches an irreversible critical point.
The fusion of Obito with the Juubi reminds that the cycle of pain and grief can lead an individual to become the threat he once sought to destroy.
alternative_seeds: If uchiha_madara had directly absorbed the Juubi without going through Obito as an intermediary, then?
If uchiha_obito had refused to become the Juubi Jinchuuriki at the last moment, then?
If the Allied Ninja Forces had successfully sealed the Juubi before its complete fusion with Obito, then?
If the Juubi had rejected Obito as a host and taken total control without a conscious host, then?
If Rin or the memory of Rin had broken Obito's decision just before the fusion, then?

hashirama_childhood_river_meetings.name_en: Childhood Meetings of Hashirama and Madara by the River
narrative_invariants: Hashirama and Madara share the same dream of a world where ninja children will not die in battle.
Their friendship temporarily transcends the ancestral rivalry between the Senju and Uchiha clans.
Hashirama initially does not know that his friend is an Uchiha, just as Madara does not know that he is a Senju.
This period solidifies Hashirama's unshakable conviction that peace is possible between enemies.
alternative_seeds: If their fathers had not interrupted their friendship, would the village have been founded earlier?
If Hashirama had known from the beginning that Madara was an Uchiha, would he have maintained this friendship?
If Madara had not lost his brothers, would his trust in Hashirama have survived?
If one of them had betrayed the other during childhood, would the village of Konoha have ever existed?

hashirama_premier_hokage_intronisation.name_en: Hashirama Senju is named the First Hokage of Konoha
narrative_invariants: The vote of the founding clans designates Hashirama rather than Madara as the first Hokage, deeply humiliating Madara.
Hashirama accepts this title in the spirit of serving peace rather than his own power.
The inauguration formalizes the shinobi governance system that will last for all future generations.
The symbolic rejection of Madara by the founding clans of Konoha sows the seed of his future betrayal.
alternative_seeds: If Madara had been named the first Hokage, would Konoha have followed a completely different path?
If Hashirama had voluntarily ceded the title to Madara to preserve their friendship, would peace have lasted?
If the system of Kage had not been created by Hashirama, how would the villages have been governed?
If Tobirama had not been present as the natural successor, would Madara have left Konoha anyway?

hashirama_donne_kyubi_mito.name_en: Hashirama entrusts the Kyubi to Mito Uzumaki as the first jinchuuriki

preconditions.value: captured by Hashirama after battle against Madara

narrative_invariants: Hashirama captures the Kyubi during his battle against Madara in the Valley of the End and becomes the first human controller.
The decision to seal the Kyubi into Mito rather than himself reveals Hashirama's absolute trust in his wife.
The distribution of the Bijuu to the villages by Hashirama is both a gesture of peace and a strategic control act.
The act establishes the tradition of jinchuuriki that will span all generations and influence the history of Naruto.
alternative_seeds: If Hashirama had sealed the Kyubi into himself, would he have survived and changed the course of history?
If the other villages had refused the Bijuu offered by Hashirama, would peace have been possible anyway?
If Mito had not been chosen as the jinchuuriki, who would have carried the Kyubi and with what consequences?
If Hashirama had destroyed the Bijuu rather than distributing them, would the shinobi world be more peaceful?

hashirama_mort_premiere_grande_guerre.name_en: Death of Hashirama Senju during the post-founding era of Konoha
narrative_invariants: Hashirama's death ends the golden age of Konoha and marks the end of the founding era.
His death leaves Tobirama alone responsible for a village in construction in a still dangerously unstable world.
The death of the Shinobi God creates a power vacuum that no one can ever fill during his lifetime.
Hashirama's legacy — his cells, his Mokuton, his vision — will continue to haunt and shape the history of shinobi long after his death.
alternative_seeds: If Hashirama had lived long enough to train the next generation himself, would Konoha have experienced the same crises?
If Hashirama had designated a successor other than Tobirama, would Konoha's policy toward the Uchiha have been different?
If the Mokuton had been passed down naturally to descendants, would Orochimaru have tried to manipulate Hashirama's cells?
If Hashirama had not died before the First Great Ninja War, would he have been able to prevent it?

hashirama_edo_tensei_quatrieme_guerre.name_en: Resurrection of Hashirama by Edo Tensei and revelation to Sasuke

preconditions.value: understanding the truth about Itachi and Konoha

narrative_invariants: Hashirama's resurrection tells Sasuke the complete truth about Konoha's founding, the dilemma with Madara, and the sacrifices made.
His recount is the catalyst that allows Sasuke to choose to defend Konoha rather than destroy it.
The gathering of the four resurrected Hokages symbolizes the continuity of the Fire's will across ages.
Hashirama confronts Madara on the battlefield, transforming their lifelong rivalry into a final confrontation under Edo Tensei.
The temporary resurrection of Hashirama confirms that his power even as a corpse animated remains unmatched among the Hokages.
alternative_seeds: If Hashirama had not convinced Sasuke, would he have fought against Konoha during the Fourth War?
If Hashirama had been able to contain Madara alone during the war, would Kaguya have been resurrected anyway?
If Hashirama had passed different information to Sasuke about the Uchiha, would the reconciliation have occurred?
If the four Hokages had not been resurrected, would Naruto have survived the war's final phase?

onoki_trains_under_mu.name_en: Onoki becomes a disciple of Mu, the Second Tsuchikage

outcomes.value: designated successor to the position of Tsuchikage

narrative_invariants: Mu is the direct master of Onoki and he transmits the Dust Release, a rare kekkei tota.
The master-disciple relationship between Mu and Onoki shapes Onoki's hard and pragmatic philosophy.
Onoki inherits the isolationist and militaristic vision of Iwagakure.
alternative_seeds: If Onoki had not mastered the Dust Release, would he have been able to become Tsuchikage?
If Mu had lived longer, would Iwa's policy have been different?
If Onoki had had another master, would he have adopted a less aggressive stance toward Konoha?

onoki_meets_hashirama_senju.name_en: Onoki meets Hashirama Senju and is marked by his power

outcomes.value: deeply marked by the power and vision of peace of Hashirama
oscillation between admiration of peace and warrior pragmatism
vivid memory of Hashirama as the ultimate reference of power

narrative_invariants: Onoki is one of the few ninjas still alive at the time of the Fourth War to have met Hashirama in person.
The encounter with Hashirama leaves Onoki an enduring ambivalence between idealism and realpolitik.
Onoki remembers having been dominated and humiliated before the power of Hashirama, which shapes his pride.
alternative_seeds: If Onoki had fully embraced Hashirama's philosophy, would Iwa have joined peace alliances earlier?
If Hashirama had not impressed Onoki, would he have developed as much determination to become stronger?
If Onoki had passed this memory to Deidara, would Deidara's fate have changed?

onoki_orders_assassination_of_obito_kabu.name_en: Onoki orders assassination missions against Konoha ninjas during the Third War

outcomes.value: orders to eliminate Konoha ninjas including genins
Tsuchikage ruthless ready to sacrifice innocents for victory
use of agents to neutralize enemy forces from within

narrative_invariants: Onoki fully assumes responsibility for the war crimes committed by Iwa, he does not deny them.
These acts constitute the heavy past that Onoki seeks to atone for during the Fourth War.
Onoki's moral rigidity in war contrasts with his evolution toward more humanism as he ages.
alternative_seeds: If Onoki had refused these missions, would Iwa have lost the Third War?
If the victims of these missions had survived, would Konoha-Iwa relations have been different after the war?
If Onoki had not had this dark past, would he have been as touched by Naruto's speech on peace?

onoki_participates_fourth_war_dust_relea.name_en: Onoki uses the Dust Release against the edo tensei during the Fourth Ninja War

outcomes.value: active combat in the front line despite his advanced age
decisive use of the Dust Release against major threats including Muu's resurrection
renewal of his determination through Naruto's example
boosted by the combat presence of the ancient Kage

narrative_invariants: Onoki chooses to fight personally rather than command from the rear, assuming his share of the burden.
He faces his own master Mu resurrected by edo tensei, symbolizing the break with his brutal past.
The old Tsuchikage embodies the transmission to the next generation of the will to protect peace.
alternative_seeds: If Onoki had been unable to fight, who would have stopped Mu's resurrection?
If Onoki had not been inspired by Naruto, would he have remained on his old convictions until the end?
If Onoki had died during this war, what impact would it have on Iwa's policy after the conflict?

onoki_creates_akuta_and_puppet_army_boru.name_en: Onoki creates the Akuta and the puppet army to protect humans, causing a crisis

preconditions.value: suffering of ninjas in past wars
artificial son created by Onoki via Particle Style

outcomes.value: creation of artificial beings composed of particles as substitutes for human soldiers
gains independence and betrays Onoki's original vision
dies sacrificing his life to destroy Ku and end the threat
major crisis between Onoki and the new Iwa leadership
testifies to the redemptive death of Onoki

narrative_invariants: Onoki dies in an attempt to fix his mistakes, his sacrifice being the logical conclusion of his redemptive arc.
The creation of Ku illustrates that even good intentions can lead to catastrophes if they ignore humanity.
Onoki passes on a final lesson to the next generation by accepting death to protect those he loves.
alternative_seeds: If Ku had not developed a separate consciousness and malice, would Onoki's project have succeeded?
If Kurotsuchi had been informed of Onoki's plans, would the crisis have been avoided?
If Onoki had survived, what role would he have played against the threats of Kara and Isshiki?

tobirama_kills_izuna_uchiha.name_en: Tobirama kills Izuna Uchiha, brother of Madara
narrative_invariants: The death of Izuna by Tobirama's hand is the original wound that permanently poisons the Senju-Uchiha relations.
Tobirama bears part of the responsibility for Madara's subsequent radicalization.
This act of war is legitimate within the context of the Warring States but marks Tobirama as a sworn enemy of the Uchiha clan.
The loss of Izuna leads Madara to transplant his brother's eyes, eventually obtaining the Rinnegan.
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had not killed Izuna, would Madara have accepted peace with the Senju without hidden bitterness?
If Izuna had survived, would the Uchiha clan have played a more stable role in the founding of Konoha?
If Madara had not obtained the Eternal Mangekyou via Izuna, would he have been able to rival Hashirama?

tobirama_becomes_second_hokage.name_en: Tobirama is designated Second Hokage after the death of Hashirama
narrative_invariants: Tobirama ascends to the title of Second Hokage not through conquest but through recognized legitimacy after his brother's death.
His takeover marks an institutional turning point: he transforms Konoha into a formal political structure.
Tobirama identifies and trains Hiruzen Sarutobi as the next Hokage, ensuring leadership continuity.
The transition from First to Second Hokage illustrates the fragility of the founders and the necessity of transmission.
alternative_seeds: If another shinobi had been designated Hokage instead of Tobirama, would Konoha's institutions have taken a different form?
If Tobirama had refused the title, would Konoha's political structure have collapsed after Hashirama's death?
If Tobirama had designated an Uchiha as successor, would the massacre of the clan have been avoided generations later?

tobirama_creates_hiraishin_and_edo_tense.name_en: Tobirama develops the Hiraishin and Edo Tensei, forbidden foundational jutsu

outcomes.value: passed down then perfected by Minato

narrative_invariants: Tobirama is the inventor of two of the most powerful and consequential jutsu in the series.
Edo Tensei, though invented by Tobirama, is later used by Orochimaru and Kabuto for massive destructions, illustrating the creator's responsibility.
The Hiraishin is later taken up and perfected by Minato Namikaze, creating a technical lineage between the Hokages.
These creations reveal that Tobirama prioritizes strategic efficiency even at the cost of ethical considerations.
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had never developed Edo Tensei, would the Fourth Ninja War have occurred in its canonical form?
If the Hiraishin had not been passed to Minato, would Konoha have survived the Kyuubi attack?
If Tobirama had sealed Edo Tensei definitively, would Orochimaru have found another way to resurrect the Hokages?

tobirama_death_kinkaku_ginkaku.name_en: Tobirama sacrifices his life to protect his shinobi against the Kumo commando

outcomes.value: heroic death sacrifice passed to history

narrative_invariants: Tobirama chooses to serve as a decoy to allow his companions to escape, dying as a responsible Hokage.
His last official act is designating Hiruzen Sarutobi as Third Hokage before sacrificing himself.
The death of Tobirama ends the founding generation of Konoha and begins the era of Hokages shaped by predecessors.
This sacrifice embodies the ninja values of Konoha: the Hokage walks ahead and dies first if necessary.
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had survived the ambush of Kinkaku and Ginkaku, would he have continued to govern Konoha or designated Hiruzen as Hokage regardless?
If another shinobi had sacrificed himself in his place, would Tobirama have influenced Konoha's institutions more?
If Tobirama had been captured rather than killed, would his Edo Tensei have been used against him while he was alive?

tobirama_founds_konoha_police_uchiha.name_en: Tobirama creates the military police of Konoha and delegates command to the Uchiha
narrative_invariants: The creation of the police by Tobirama is interpreted by the Uchiha as a honorable way to distance them from strategic decisions in the village.
This political act lays the structural foundations for the Uchiha resentment that culminates decades later in the massacre.
Tobirama acts out of distrust toward the Uchiha, openly expressing it, creating a mutual cycle of defiance.
The police institution, though legitimate on the surface, functions as a cage that fuels Uchiha alienation.
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had integrated the Uchiha into military command rather than the police, would the clan have developed a stronger sense of belonging to Konoha?
If the Uchiha had refused the police post, would the rift with Konoha have been more immediate?
If Hashirama had been alive during this decision, would he have approved or opposed it?

sakura_tsunade_apprenticeship_start.name_en: Sakura becomes Tsunade the Sannin's apprentice
narrative_invariants: Sakura is chosen by Tsunade due to her exceptional chakra control
This event marks the transformation of Sakura from a passive character into an active and determined kunoichi, determining her path
The master-apprentice relationship between Tsunade and Sakura reflects that between Jiraiya and Naruto
Sakura gains access to a medical and physical training regimen without equivalent for a genin
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had refused to take Sakura as a student, what level would she have reached on her own?
If Sakura had followed Jiraiya or Orochimaru, what ninja would she have become?
If another Sannin had trained Sakura, would the dynamics of Team 7 have changed?

sakura_punches_inner_gates_sasori.name_en: Sakura and Chiyo defeat Sasori of the Red Sand
narrative_invariants: Sakura proves for the first time she can defeat an Akatsuki member without direct help from Naruto or Sasuke
The victory over Sasori requires both the medical antidote and the physical power from Tsunade's training
Chiyo sacrifices her life to rescue Gaara, leaving Sakura heartbroken and strengthened in her medical vocation
This victory establishes Sakura as a top fighter in the eyes of her peers
alternative_seeds: If Sakura had not mastered Tsunade's antidotes, would she have survived Sasori's poison?
If Chiyo had not been present, could Sakura have defeated Sasori alone?
If Sasori had survived, what information about the Akatsuki would he have revealed?

sakura_fake_confession_sasuke.name_en: Sakura makes a false confession of love to Naruto to stop him
narrative_invariants: Sakura tries to take responsibility for stopping Sasuke on her own to protect Naruto
The false confession reveals the depth of Sakura's internal conflict between love and ninja duty
Naruto and Kakashi find out the lie, highlighting Sakura's inability to hide her emotions
This event marks a painful turning point in Sakura's relationship with Naruto
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had believed Sakura, would he have abandoned his promise to bring back Sasuke?
If Sakura had actually killed Sasuke, what would have been the reaction of Naruto and the village?
If Kakashi had not intercepted Sakura, would she have died facing Sasuke?

sakura_yin_seal_activation_war.name_en: Sakura activates the Yin Seal and provides mass care during the Fourth Great War
narrative_invariants: The activation of the Yin Seal places Sakura at the same level as Tsunade on the medical and physical scale
Sakura becomes the central medical figure of the shinobi alliance during the war
This transformation concludes the initiation arc of her training initiated by her apprenticeship under Tsunade
Sakura demonstrates that she has surpassed the expectations of a genin to become an indispensable figure on a global scale
alternative_seeds: If Sakura had not accumulated the Yin Seal early enough, how many additional ninjas would have been lost?
If Tsunade had been absent from the war, would Sakura have taken over the complete medical command?
If Sakura had used the Yin Seal before the war, would she have saved Neji?

sasuke_sakura_mariage_voyage.name_en: Marriage of Sasuke and Sakura followed by a journey of expiation
narrative_invariants: The marriage concludes Sakura's emotional arc beginning from her childhood feelings towards Sasuke
Sasuke accepts Sakura at his side despite his choice to go on a solitary journey of expiation
Sarada is conceived and born outside of Konoha during this journey, establishing the foundations of her future identity questions
The union of Sakura and Sasuke symbolizes the reconciliation of the clans' values and love despite the war
alternative_seeds: If Sakura had not followed Sasuke on the journey, would Sarada have had a different childhood in Konoha?
If Sasuke had refused the marriage, would Sakura have continued her medical life alone?
If Sarada had been born in Konoha, would her doubts about identity have ever existed?

deidara_defects_iwagakure_joins_akatsuki.name_en: Deidara defects from Iwagakure and is forced to join the Akatsuki
narrative_invariants: Deidara is defeated by Itachi and his Sharingan before being forcibly recruited into the Akatsuki
The defeat against the Sharingan plants a lasting hatred in Deidara toward the Uchiha and their dojutsu
Deidara is the youngest member of the Akatsuki at the time of his integration
alternative_seeds: If Deidara had refused to join the Akatsuki despite the defeat, what fate would he have faced?
If Deidara had not been defeated by the Sharingan, would he have developed an obsession as intense toward the Uchiha?
If Iwagakure had protected Deidara from forced recruitment, would it have sparked a conflict between the Akatsuki and the village?

deidara_sasori_capturent_gaara.name_en: Deidara and Sasori capture Gaara, jinchuuriki of Ichibi
narrative_invariants: Deidara loses his left arm in the battle with Gaara but still wins the fight
The capture of Gaara triggers the mission to rescue Naruto and the Suna
This is the first major visible act of the Akatsuki's hunt for jinchuuriki in the main canon
alternative_seeds: If Gaara had managed to repel Deidara, would the extraction of the jinchuuriki mission have been delayed?
If Deidara had not lost his arm, would he have developed more advanced techniques to face Sasuke later?
If the Kazekage had been escorted during his return, would the capture have been avoided?

deidara_combat_et_mort_sasuke.name_en: Deidara fights Sasuke and sacrifices himself with C0 without killing him
narrative_invariants: Deidara uses his ultimate technique C0, turning himself into a bomb, but Sasuke survives thanks to Manda summoned as a shield
Deidara's death is a chosen and spectacular one, consistent with his philosophy of ephemeral art
Deidara dies without proving his superiority over the Sharingan, leaving his inner conflict unresolved
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had not had Manda to absorb the explosion, would the Itachi arc have been possible?
If Deidara had chosen a strategic retreat instead of dying, what role would he have played in the war?
If Deidara had found a way to neutralize the Sharingan, would his artistic philosophy have been transformed?

deidara_edo_tensei_guerre_ninja.name_en: Deidara is resurrected by the Edo Tensei during the Fourth Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Deidara is one of the many shinobi resurrected by Kabuto to strengthen the Akatsuki's army during the war
Even under Edo Tensei, Deidara's personality and techniques remain intact, illustrating that his identity is inseparable from his art
The resurrection highlights the lasting impact of Akatsuki members on the global conflict even after their deaths
alternative_seeds: If Deidara had been resurrected earlier in the war, could he have changed the outcome of certain key battles?
If Deidara had found Sasuke under Edo Tensei, would the duel have ended differently?
If Kabuto had not been able to obtain Deidara's remains, which other Akatsuki members would have been prioritized?

minato_becomes_fourth_hokage.name_en: Minato Namikaze is named Fourth Hokage of Konohagakure
narrative_invariants: Minato is recognized as the most powerful ninja of his generation and becomes Hokage.
His appointment confirms the absolute trust of Konoha in his abilities after the Third War.
Hiruzen Sarutobi hands over leadership of the village before taking the title again later.
alternative_seeds: If Orochimaru had been chosen instead of Minato, how would Konoha's history have diverged?
If Minato had refused the title, who would have faced Kurama during the attack?
If Minato had lived, would Hiruzen have ever taken the role of Third Hokage?

minato_hiraishin_no_jutsu_mastery.name_en: Minato perfects the Hiraishin and decimates Iwa's army during the Third Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Minato alone neutralizes an entire Iwagakure army, an unprecedented feat in the war.
The Hiraishin no Jutsu, originally created by the Second Hokage, is pushed to its ultimate form by Minato.
Iwa issues an immediate death order against any ninja crossing Minato, illustrating the terror he inspires.
This event establishes his legend and nickname as the Konoha Yellow Flash.
alternative_seeds: If Minato had not mastered the Hiraishin, would Konoha have won the Third Great Ninja War?
If Tobirama had not created the Hiraishin, would Minato have developed an equivalent technique?
If Iwa had succeeded in killing Minato during the war, would Obito have been able to manipulate Kurama's attack?

minato_trains_kakashi_obito_rin.name_en: Minato takes charge of the original Team 7: Kakashi, Obito, and Rin as jonin-sensei
narrative_invariants: Minato forms a master-apprentice bond with Kakashi, Obito, and Rin, the central trio of the entire saga.
Minato's teaching directly influences Kakashi's philosophy as a future sensei of the Team 7 of Naruto.
The dynamic of this team foreshadows that of Naruto, Sasuke, and Sakura, establishing a recurring motif.
alternative_seeds: If Minato had been a more strict sensei, would Obito have developed the same emotional bond with Rin?
If Minato had taught Kakashi the Hiraishin instead of the Chidori, which shinobi would have become Kakashi?
If Minato had been present during the mission at the Kannabi Bridge, would he have prevented Obito's apparent death?

minato_seals_kurama_naruto_birth.name_en: Minato seals half of Kurama into Naruto and sacrifices his life to protect Konoha
narrative_invariants: Minato consciously sacrifices his life and Kushina's to save Konoha and give Naruto an weapon against the future.
The Yin-Yang seal divides Kurama into two halves, a strategic choice that has decisive consequences in the Fourth War.
Minato leaves a part of his consciousness in the seal of Naruto to guide his son when the time comes.
This act makes Naruto an orphan who will grow under the weight of the ignorance of his origins and the burden of the Kyuubi.
alternative_seeds: If Minato had sealed the entirety of Kurama into Naruto instead of half, would Naruto have survived?
If Minato had survived by using another sealing technique, how would he have raised Naruto?
If Minato had managed to identify and neutralize Obito before the sealing, would Kurama have ever attacked Konoha?
If Hiruzen had revealed Naruto's parents' identity from childhood, how would Naruto's psychology have changed?

minato_reanimation_fourth_war.name_en: Minato is reanimated by Orochimaru and fights alongside Naruto during the Fourth Great War
narrative_invariants: Minato and Naruto reunite as father and son on the battlefield, achieving a emotional and symbolic reunion.
Minato, regaining the Yin half of Kurama released from the Shinigami, can access the full Kurama mode.
The presence of Minato validates the path Naruto has taken and confirms that the son has surpassed the father.
Minato confronts Obito and understands the full extent of his indirect responsibility in creating Tobi's mask.
alternative_seeds: If Minato had not been reanimated, who would have contained Obito as the Juubi Jinchuuriki?
If Minato had spoken longer with Naruto during the war, what additional revelations would he have conveyed?
If Minato had survived the war in a permanent form, how would his presence have changed Konoha's policy?

nagato_awakens_rinnegan.name_en: Nagato awakens the Rinnegan after the murder of his parents
narrative_invariants: Nagato's Rinnegan is in reality the one implanted by Madara Uchiha at birth.
The awakening occurs during a fit of rage and pain following his parents' death.
This event solidifies Nagato's belief that war is an absurd cycle of suffering.
Nagato unintentionally kills the soldiers responsible, marking the beginning of his destructive capacity.
alternative_seeds: If Nagato had not awakened the Rinnegan that day, would Madara have recovered his eyes sooner?
If his parents had survived, would Nagato have ever joined Yahiko's quest for peace?
If Nagato had understood the origin of his eyes as a child, would he have rejected Madara's plan?

nagato_forme_les_six_paths_of_pain.name_en: Nagato forms the Six Paths of Pain from corpses
narrative_invariants: Nagato uses the body of Yahiko as Devapath, betraying and honoring both the memory of his friend.
The Six Paths of Pain are corpses of shinobi each carrying Nagato's chakra receivers.
Nagato remains hidden, his body too damaged to fight directly.
The transformation into Pain marks the definitive break between Yahiko's ideal and Nagato's method.
alternative_seeds: If Nagato had used another body as Devapath, would his psychology towards Yahiko have been different?
If Nagato had been able to move himself, would he have abandoned the Six Paths system?
If Konan had opposed the use of Yahiko's body, would their rupture have been possible?

nagato_tue_jiraiya_combat.name_en: Nagato confronts and kills Jiraiya via the Six Paths of Pain

outcomes.value: teacher killed by his own student

narrative_invariants: Nagato must deploy the six paths simultaneously to defeat Jiraiya, proving the latter's power.
Jiraiya recognizes Pain as his former student and dies with this knowledge without fully transmitting it.
The murder of Jiraiya by his own student symbolizes the failure of the master-student cycle to break hatred.
Nagato shows no apparent pleasure in this victory, highlighting the tragic trajectories of both characters.
alternative_seeds: If Jiraiya had successfully identified Nagato as Pain and escaped, would Naruto have prepared a different counterplan?
If Nagato had let Jiraiya live in exchange for information, would the war's balance have changed?
If Jiraiya had known from the start that Pain was Nagato, would he have convinced him to abandon it?

nagato_ressuscite_konoha_rinne_tensei.name_en: Nagato uses Rinne Tensei to resurrect all the dead of Konoha and dies
narrative_invariants: Nagato chooses to believe in the cycle of peace carried by Naruto rather than his own vision of universal suffering.
Rinne Tensei consumes all of his vital energy, making his death certain and conscious.
Nagato dies having passed on the torch of hope to Naruto, closing the narrative loop with Jiraiya.
His redemption does not deny his crimes but accepts them as the cost of his wrong path.
Konan accepts Nagato's decision despite her own suffering, honoring their bond to the end.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had failed to convince Nagato, would he have captured the Kyuubi and complete Tobi's plan?
If Rinne Tensei had not been mortal, would Nagato have become an active ally in the war?
If Nagato had survived, what role would he have played in the Fourth Ninja War?
If Konan had tried to stop Nagato to keep him alive, would it have changed their final relationship?

naruto_kyuubi_chakra_control_year16.name_en: Naruto masters the chakra of Kyuubi in the cloak version 2
narrative_invariants: The chakra of Kyuubi without cooperation consumes Naruto's body
Kurama's hatred remains an obstacle even in the cloak form
This form is a temporary solution before true cooperation
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had learned to cooperate with Kurama earlier, would the cloak version 2 have been necessary?
If Naruto had lost control completely during this phase, what would he have become?
If Yamato had not been present to contain Naruto, the damage would have been irreversible?

naruto_meets_mother_kushina_kurama_seal.name_en: Naruto meets Kushina's spirit and learns the truth about his birth
narrative_invariants: Kushina sacrificed part of her chakra to protect Naruto on the day of his birth
Naruto learns that his parents loved him deeply despite their death
The meeting with Kushina triggers the conscious control of the seal
alternative_seeds: If the Minato seal had not preserved Kushina's chakra, would Naruto have descended into Kyuubi's madness?
If Naruto had learned about his parents' identity earlier, would his motivation have been different?
If Kushina had not transmitted her memories, would Naruto have mentally defeated Kurama?

naruto_kurama_alliance_year16.name_en: Naruto and Kurama form an alliance and share their chakra
narrative_invariants: Naruto breaks the cycle of hatred by accepting Kurama's pain without hostility
The alliance is based on mutual trust and not domination
This cooperation is the foundation of the Kurama chakra mode that will be used during the war
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had tried to dominate Kurama like the previous jinchuuriki, would the alliance have been possible?
If Kurama had refused cooperation, would Naruto have survived the Four Nations War?
If the alliance had been concluded earlier, would Nagato have been saved?

naruto_sage_training_myobokuzan_year15.name_en: Naruto trains in the sage mode at Myobokuzan after Jiraiya's death
narrative_invariants: Naruto is the only human to master the sage mode without being petrified thanks to his exceptional chakra
Jiraiya's death is the primary emotional motivation for this training
Naruto develops a unique method of natural chakra accumulation via clones to compensate for his inability to remain still
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had not mastered the sage mode, would he have defeated Pain?
If Jiraiya had survived, would the sage mode training have taken a different form?
If Naruto had failed the training and been petrified, who would have faced Pain?

naruto_hagoromo_meeting_year16.name_en: Naruto meets the Sage of the Six Paths and receives his chakra
narrative_invariants: Naruto is confirmed as the reincarnation of Asura Otsutsuki, mirror of Sasuke-Indra
Hagoromo transmits his chakra to both Naruto and Sasuke to defeat Kaguya
This meeting reveals the cosmic meaning of the Naruto-Sasuke conflict as an eternal repetition
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had refused Hagoromo's chakra, would he have defeated Madara and Kaguya?
If Sasuke had been the only one to receive Hagoromo's chakra, would he have tried to destroy the shinobi world?
If Naruto had learned earlier that he was Asura's reincarnation, would that have changed his relationship with Sasuke?

hagoromo_defeats_seals_kaguya.name_en: Hagoromo and Hamura seal Kaguya in the Jubi

outcomes.value: departed to the moon to guard Kaguya's body

narrative_invariants: Hagoromo is the first jinchuriki in history
Sealing Kaguya requires both brothers acting together
Hamura separates from Hagoromo after victory to guard the moon
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had failed to seal Kaguya, would the Earth have been destroyed?
If Hamura had stayed on Earth, would the Otsutsuki clan have survived differently?
If only one brother had been sufficient, what would have been the fate of the other?

hagoromo_creates_ninshuu_spreads_chakra.name_en: Hagoromo found the ninshu and shared chakra with humanity

preconditions.value: former jinchuriki after splitting ten tails

narrative_invariants: The ninshu is created to connect human souls through chakra
Hagoromo did not want chakra to be used as a weapon of war
The corruption of the ninshu into ninjutsu is an unintended consequence by Hagoromo
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had not shared chakra, would humanity have evolved differently?
If the ninshu had not been corrupted into ninjutsu, would peace have been achieved during his lifetime?
If Hagoromo had chosen a single universal successor, would clan wars have occurred?

hagoromo_splits_ten_tails_creates_bijuu.name_en: Hagoromo splits the Jubi into nine Bijuu and sends the Juubi without chakra to the moon
narrative_invariants: The creation of the nine Bijuu is a deliberate act of Hagoromo to avoid the concentration of the Jubi's chakra
The body of the Jubi without chakra becomes the Gedo Mazo sealed on the moon
Each Bijuu receives a name and a distinct consciousness from Hagoromo
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had kept the Jubi sealed within him, what could he have achieved?
If the Gedo Mazo had not been sent to the moon, would Madara have been able to summon it as easily?
If the Bijuu had been a different number, would the jinchuriki and power balances have changed?

hagoromo_indra_asura_conflict_curse.name_en: Hagoromo designates Asura as successor, triggering the rivalry with Indra
narrative_invariants: Hagoromo's choice of Asura is motivated by the belief in peace through bonds rather than force
The rivalry between Indra and Asura reemerges in cycles until the era of Naruto and Sasuke
Hagoromo only realizes late the manipulative role of Black Zetsu in Indra's fall
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had chosen Indra, would the cycle of violence have taken a different form?
If Hagoromo had equally passed his abilities to both sons, would the rivalry have been avoided?
If Black Zetsu had not manipulated Indra, would the succession have been accepted in peace?

hagoromo_appears_naruto_sasuke_war.name_en: The spirit of Hagoromo appears to Naruto and Sasuke and entrusts them with his powers
narrative_invariants: Hagoromo recognizes in Naruto and Sasuke the reincarnations of Asura and Indra capable of breaking the cycle
The transmission of Hagoromo's powers is the ultimate response to Kaguya/Black Zetsu's plan
Hagoromo confesses his responsibility for the suffering caused by his family and heritage
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had chosen others than Naruto and Sasuke, would the outcome have been different?
If Hagoromo had revealed Black Zetsu's existence earlier, lives could have been saved?
If Naruto had refused Hagoromo's powers, how would the war have ended?

hinata_disinherited_hyuga.name_en: Hinata disinherited as the heir of the Hyuga clan
narrative_invariants: Hinata's perceived weakness deprives her of her clan heritage from childhood.
The Hyuga clan values power and firmness at the expense of compassion.
This disinheritance shapes the central identity wound of Hinata.
Hanabi becomes the heir despite being her younger sister.
Hinata is reduced to proving her worth outside clan expectations.
alternative_seeds: If Hiashi had supported Hinata, would she have developed her own style of Jyuken?
If Hinata had remained heir, would she have had the same bond with Naruto?
If the clan had valued compassion, would the fate of the Hyuga clan have changed during the war?

hinata_vs_neji_chunin_exam.name_en: Hinata faces Neji during the Chunin exams and refuses to give up
narrative_invariants: Hinata refuses to give up even facing certain defeat, influenced by Naruto's example.
This is the first time Hinata publicly affirms who she is and what she wants to become.
Neji embodies the fatalistic philosophy of the Hyuga clan that Hinata implicitly rejects.
Naruto is moved and motivated by Hinata's determination, mirroring his own.
The confrontation reveals the deep divide between the main and secondary branches of the Hyuga clan.
alternative_seeds: If Hinata had given up to Neji, would Naruto have been as determined to beat her?
If Hinata had won, would the clan's view of her have changed?
If Neji had not been so cruel, would the subsequent reconciliation have weighed as much?

hinata_confesses_naruto_pain_arc.name_en: Hinata confesses her feelings to Naruto and confronts Pain to defend him
narrative_invariants: Hinata chooses to fight Pain knowing she will lose, only to protect Naruto.
This is the first explicit confession of her romantic feelings in the canon.
Her injury is the direct trigger for Naruto's transformation into six tails.
Hinata transcends her fear for the first time without hesitation against a powerful enemy.
This act of sacrifice defines the peak of Hinata's bravery arc in the original series.
alternative_seeds: If Hinata had not intervened, would Naruto have found another way to free himself?
If Naruto had been aware of his feelings earlier, would the war arc have been different?
If Pain had killed Hinata definitively, how would Naruto have evolved?

hinata_red_scarf_last_movie.name_en: Hinata weaves a red scarf for Naruto and must choose between him and her sister
narrative_invariants: The red scarf woven by Hinata since childhood symbolizes her patient and silent love for Naruto.
Hinata chooses to approach Toneri to save her sister, which deeply wounds Naruto.
Naruto finally understands his feelings for Hinata during this mission.
The mission against Toneri marks the transition from unilateral love to a reciprocal relationship.
This is the narrative resolution of the romantic relationship between Naruto and Hinata.
alternative_seeds: If Hinata had not sacrificed the scarf, would Naruto have understood her feelings differently?
If Toneri had convinced Hinata, would the moon have been destroyed?
If Naruto had rejected Hinata, how would the Hyuga clan have evolved?

kaguya_betrays_otsutsuki_clan.name_en: Kaguya betrays the Otsutsuki clan and eats the God Tree fruit
narrative_invariants: Kaguya is the first human to wield chakra on Earth.
Her betrayal of her clan is the origin of all ninja lineages.
The God Tree fruit was intended for the Otsutsuki clan, not humanity.
alternative_seeds: If Kaguya had not eaten the fruit, would the Otsutsuki clan have invaded the Earth?
If another human had eaten the fruit before her, would the ninja lineage be different?
If Kaguya had willingly shared the chakra, would she have avoided being sealed?

kaguya_merges_with_god_tree.name_en: Kaguya fuses with the Divine Tree and becomes the Ten-Tailed Beast
narrative_invariants: The Ten-Tailed Beast is in reality Kaguya's original form.
Kaguya's fusion with the Divine Tree is the trigger for the battle against her sons.
This is Kaguya's ultimate act to reclaim all the chakra she dispersed.
alternative_seeds: If Kaguya had not fused with the tree, would her sons have been able to coexist with her?
If the fusion had been incomplete, would Kaguya have retained her consciousness?
If no one had sealed the Jubi, would the world have survived?

kaguya_sealed_by_hagoromo_hamura.name_en: Kaguya is sealed by her sons Hagoromo and Hamura in the Moon
narrative_invariants: Kaguya's sealing by her own sons is the foundation of all shinobi mythology.
This act grants Hagoromo the title of Sage of the Six Paths.
The Ten-Tailed Beast is divided into nine Foreign Beasts after Kaguya's sealing.
alternative_seeds: If Hamura had not participated in the sealing, would Hagoromo have been able to defeat Kaguya alone?
If Kaguya had been destroyed instead of sealed, would the chakra have disappeared?
If the Moon had not been used as a prison, would Kaguya have been freed sooner?

kaguya_controls_humans_infinite_tsukuyom.name_en: Kaguya subjugates humanity with the Tsukuyomi Infinite and creates the White Zetsu
narrative_invariants: The White Zetsu are the human victims of Kaguya's original Tsukuyomi Infinite
Kaguya had already used the Tsukuyomi Infinite before Madara attempted to replicate it
Kaguya's chakra collection plan is the original version of the Eye of the Moon project
alternative_seeds: If the White Zetsu had retained their consciousness, could they have resisted Kaguya?
If Madara had known the White Zetsu were humans, would he have changed his plan?
If the original Tsukuyomi Infinite had failed, would Kaguya have found another method?

kaguya_black_zetsu_creation_revelation.name_en: Revelation that Kaguya created Black Zetsu to orchestrate her own return
narrative_invariants: Black Zetsu is a creation of Kaguya and not Hagoromo's will as everyone believed
The entire history of the Uchiha and Senju clans was manipulated by Black Zetsu for Kaguya's benefit
Madara Uchiha was a simple pawn in Kaguya's resurrection plan
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had discovered Black Zetsu was Kaguya's creation, would he have eliminated it sooner?
If Madara had known he was Kaguya's pawn, would he have changed his plan?
If Black Zetsu had failed in his manipulation, would Kaguya have found another way to resurrect?

kakashi_father_sakumo_suicide.name_en: Suicide of Sakumo Hatake, the White Crocodile of Konoha
narrative_invariants: Sakumo abandons a mission to save his comrades and is ostracized by all of Konoha.
The social shame pushes Sakumo to commit suicide in front of young Kakashi.
This event shapes young Kakashi's obsession with rules and rejection of compassion in favor of duty.
This trauma will later be reversed by Obito's lesson on the value of comrades.
alternative_seeds: If Konoha had honored Sakumo for saving his comrades, would Kakashi have developed the same moral rigor?
If Kakashi had understood his father's sacrifice earlier, would he have been able to save Rin?
If Sakumo had survived, what kind of ninja would Kakashi have become?

kakashi_becomes_chunin_prodigy.name_en: Kakashi becomes Chunin at six years old, recognized prodigy of Konoha
narrative_invariants: Kakashi is one of the youngest ninjas in Konoha's history to become chunin.
His rapid progression contrasts with his emotional isolation after his father's death.
His prodigy reputation attracts the attention of Minato Namikaze, who takes him under his wing.
alternative_seeds: If Kakashi hadn't been a prodigy, would he have been placed under Minato's tutelage?
If his progression had been slower, would he have developed more social bonds as a child?

kakashi_receives_sharingan_from_obito.name_en: Obito offers his Sharingan to Kakashi before being crushed to death
narrative_invariants: Obito transmits his Sharingan to Kakashi as a farewell gift and recognition of his value as a comrade.
This event is the pivotal identity turning point for Kakashi, who becomes the Copy Ninja.
The guilt tied to Obito's death marks Kakashi for life as a ninja.
Obito's Sharingan will eventually be returned to its original owner during the Fourth Ninja World War.
alternative_seeds: If Kakashi hadn't received the Sharingan, would he have developed the Chidori without this additional vision?
If Obito hadn't survived in secret, would Kakashi's guilt have been different?
If Kakashi had refused the Sharingan, how would Obito have lived his false death?

kakashi_loses_sharingan_gets_two_mangeky.name_en: Kakashi loses the Sharingan of Obito and temporarily receives two Mangekyo Sharingan
narrative_invariants: Obito's spirit transfers his chakra to Kakashi to allow him to manipulate two Mangekyo Sharingan and summon a complete Susanoo.
This is the only time a non-Uchiha uses Susanoo in the canon.
After the war, Kakashi permanently loses the Sharingan and regains his two normal eyes.
This event symbolically closes the bond between Kakashi and Obito, with the donation being returned to its donor.
alternative_seeds: If Kakashi had kept the Sharingan after the war, how would his role as Hokage have been perceived?
If Kakashi had been able to use Susanoo earlier in the war, how would battles have turned differently?
If Obito's spirit had not been able to transfer its eyes, would Kakashi have survived the battle against Kaguya?

kakashi_anbu_captain_era.name_en: Kakashi joins the ANBU and becomes captain under Hiruzen's orders
narrative_invariants: Kakashi joins the ANBU to escape his pain after the deaths of Obito, Rin, and Minato.
During this period, Kakashi supervises the young Itachi Uchiha within the ANBU.
Hiruzen decides to remove Kakashi from the ANBU and assign him as a sensei to genin to give him a human purpose in life.
Kakashi's ANBU period is marked by brutality and emotional detachment that scares his colleagues.
alternative_seeds: If Kakashi had remained in the ANBU, would he have been able to teach the values of teamwork to Team 7?
If Kakashi had been able to prevent the Uchiha massacre as Itachi's supervisor, would Sasuke's story have changed?
If Hiruzen had not removed Kakashi from the ANBU, who would have trained Naruto, Sasuke, and Sakura?

asura_born_no_talent.name_en: Birth of Asura without apparent special talent
narrative_invariants: Asura is born without the natural genius of his brother Indra and must compensate through effort and connection to others.
The foundational contrast between the two brothers establishes the duality of chakra that will permeate all subsequent generations.
alternative_seeds: If Asura had been the elder prodigy, would Indra have developed the same jealousy?
If Hagoromo had had a single son, would the duality of Senju-Uchiha exist?

asura_receives_ninshu_journey.name_en: Asura goes on a mission to help an isolated village and discovers the power of bonds
narrative_invariants: Asura discovers that his power does not come from innate talent but from love and solidarity with others.
This revelation shapes the philosophy that will oppose Asura to Indra for all future generations.
alternative_seeds: If Asura had failed this mission, would he have adopted Indra's vision on individual power?
If Asura had not formed these bonds, would ninshu have become a different form of ninjutsu?

hagoromo_chooses_asura_heir.name_en: Hagoromo designates Asura as his heir to his teachings instead of Indra
narrative_invariants: Hagoromo's choice in favor of Asura breaks the fraternal bond and triggers a cycle of hatred passed from reincarnation to reincarnation.
This event is the direct source of the Senju-Uchiha conflict and the rivalry between Naruto and Sasuke centuries later.
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had designated Indra or shared his heritage equally, would the war of the clans have been avoided?
If Asura had refused the heritage of brotherhood, would history have changed?

indra_attacks_asura_first_battle.name_en: Indra attacks Asura: First Fratricidal Battle of the Otsutsuki
narrative_invariants: The first battle between the two Otsutsuki brothers establishes the pattern that will repeat through the ages: power of hatred against the strength of bonds.
Asura, driven by necessity, awakens a power comparable to Indra's by relying on his allies and not brute force.
alternative_seeds: If Asura had been defeated in this first confrontation, would the reincarnation cycle have started differently?
If Indra had accepted Hagoromo's decision, would peace between their descendants have been possible?

asura_reincarnation_cycle_begins.name_en: Death of Asura and the Beginning of the Cycle of Reincarnation of His Chakra
narrative_invariants: Asura's death does not end the conflict but perpetuates it through the reincarnation of his chakra in the Senju and Uzumaki descendants.
Asura is recognized as the founder of the Senju and Uzumaki clans, making Naruto his ultimate spiritual heir.
Hagoromo's wish for his sons to reconcile one day gives profound meaning to the entire Naruto saga.
alternative_seeds: If Asura had survived and reconciled Indra while alive, would the cycle of hatred have been broken before it began?
If Asura's chakra had not reincarnated, would Naruto have accessed the Six Paths?
If the Senju and Uzumaki clans had shared their origin, would they have acted differently during the Clan Wars?

hanzo_defeats_sannin_names_them.name_en: Hanzō Fights the Future Sannins and Bestows Their Legendary Title

preconditions.value: leader of Amegakure, most feared shinobi of his time
genin or young jonin, present on the battlefield
shinobi of Konoha on a war mission
shinobi of Konoha on a war mission

outcomes.value: only shinobi to have spared enemies, enhancing his legendary status
confirmed as dominant force in the shinobi world

narrative_invariants: Hanzō is so powerful that sparing enemies is a mark of honor rather than weakness.
The title of Sannin is imposed from the outside by an enemy, not granted by Konoha.
This event permanently shapes Jiraiya, Tsunade, and Orochimaru as an extraordinary trio.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzō had killed the three at that moment, would Konoha's history have followed the same course?
If the Sannins had seriously wounded Hanzō, would his subsequent paranoia have been less intense?
If Hanzō had named other shinobi as Sannins, would that have affected Jiraiya's legitimacy as Naruto's sensei?

hanzo_poisons_himself_salamander_pact.name_en: Hanzō Concludes a Pact with the Great Salamander and Draws His Mortal Poison at the Cost of His Own Life

outcomes.value: venom implant under the skin, making his breath lethal
must always wear a mask or antidote to survive his own poison
unique venom in the world, nearly unbeatable in direct combat

narrative_invariants: Hanzō's power is inseparable from a personal sacrifice and permanent vulnerability.
Hanzō illustrates that mastering poison arts means living with death within oneself.
The pact with the Salamander is the foundation of his legend and nickname.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzō had refused the pact and sought another path, could he have united Ame without resorting to terror?
If his antidote had been discovered by an enemy, would his combat supremacy have collapsed?
If Nagato had mastered the same poison technique, would the final duel have had a different outcome?

hanzo_dangzo_trap_akatsuki_proto.name_en: Hanzō Allies with Danzo to Set a Trap for the Peaceful Akatsuki of Yahiko and Force Nagato to Kill His Friend

preconditions.value: leader of the original Akatsuki, growing pacifist movement
founder member of the Akatsuki, bearer of the Rinnegan
ruler of Ame, threatened by the rising popularity of the Akatsuki
founder member of the Akatsuki, captured as a hostage

outcomes.value: deep trauma, tilting toward hatred and viewing pain as the only peace
temporarily consolidated on Ame but created an absolute enemy
transformed into a criminal organization under Pain/Nagato
birth of the concept Pain as a direct response to Hanzō's betrayal

narrative_invariants: Yahiko's death at Nagato's hands is the turning point that transforms Nagato into Pain.
Hanzō destroys the only true chance for peace in Ame by eliminating his most idealistic opponent.
The Hanzō-Danzo alliance shows that two corrupt powers can converge against a common ideal.
This trap makes Hanzō the direct and indirect cause of Pain's attack on Konoha years later.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzō had integrated the Akatsuki instead of destroying it, would Ame have become a real peace power?
If Nagato had refused to hold the kunai, what path would his life have taken without the weight of Yahiko's death?
If Danzo had betrayed Hanzō at this moment instead of cooperating, would Ame's history have diverged?
If Konan had been killed instead of Yahiko, would Nagato have reacted the same way?

hanzo_death_by_pain.name_en: Pain tracks and kills Hanzō, exterminating his family and close ones to erase his regime

preconditions.value: weakened but still nominal leader of Amegakure
revenge for Yahiko's death and the annihilation of the original Akatsuki

outcomes.value: completely eradicated to prevent future revenge
shifted to Pain's theocracy, recognized as the village's god
completed against Hanzō, reinforcing his belief that pain and fear are the only tools of peace
venerate Pain as a divine liberator who overthrew the tyrant

narrative_invariants: Hanzō's death closes the narrative loop opened during the trap set for Yahiko.
Pain does not merely kill Hanzō; he erases all traces of his lineage, refusing any cycle of revenge.
Hanzō's fall shows that even the most feared legend can be surpassed by the generation it has itself forged.
This death solidifies Pain's myth as a living deity among Amegakure's inhabitants.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzō had sought help from Konoha or another village to protect himself from Pain, would that have triggered a war earlier?
If Hanzō had sincerely apologized to Nagato, would Pain have spared his life?
If Hanzō had survived, would he have become an unexpected ally against Obito and Madara during the Fourth Great War?
If the extermination of his close ones had failed, would a heir of Hanzō have tried to avenge his death against Pain?

obito_rescued_by_madara.name_en: Obito saves and is adopted by Madara Uchiha
narrative_invariants: Madara had planned to use Obito as a successor before meeting him
The rescue is not an act of kindness but a calculated manipulation
Hashirama's cells implanted in Obito will grant him unique abilities
Obito initially believes Madara is trying to help him sincerely
alternative_seeds: If Madara had not been watching the battlefield, would Obito have really died?
If Obito had refused to follow Madara, who would have inherited the Mugen Tsukuyomi plan?
If Hashirama's cells had not been compatible, would Obito have survived?

obito_awakens_sharingan_rin_kidnapping.name_en: Obito awakens his Sharingan to rescue Rin captured by Kirigakure
narrative_invariants: The awakening of Obito's Sharingan is directly tied to his love for Rin and his determination to protect his comrades
This event establishes Obito's moral code before his later corruption
The contrast between this Obito and the future Tobi is the tragic heart of his character
alternative_seeds: If Obito had not awakened his Sharingan here, would he have survived the war?
If Rin had not been kidnapped, would the mission have ended without Obito's presumed death?
If Kakashi had been able to rescue Rin alone, what role would Obito have played in the aftermath?

obito_gives_sharingan_to_kakashi.name_en: Obito offers his left Sharingan to Kakashi before appearing to die
narrative_invariants: The donation of the Sharingan is the founding act of Kakashi Hatake's legend
Obito gives away his most precious possession to the one he considered his rival
This apparent sacrifice shapes Kakashi's sense of duty toward his comrades for decades
The physical separation of Obito's Sharingan will be at the heart of the final arc
alternative_seeds: If Obito had kept his Sharingan, would Kakashi have become the recognized ninja he is?
If someone else had witnessed the scene, would Obito's death have been better verified?
If Kakashi had refused the eye, how would Obito have lived with his presumed death?

obito_manipulates_fox_attack_konoha.name_en: Obito takes control of the Kyuubi and triggers the attack on Konoha
narrative_invariants: Obito is directly responsible for the deaths of Naruto's parents and the lonely childhood of the hero
This act transforms Obito from a war victim into an actor in the misfortune of an entire generation
The manipulation of the Kyuubi proves that Obito already masters the Mangekyou Sharingan at this stage
Minato identifies Obito as a national threat before dying
alternative_seeds: If Minato had not been able to contain the Kyuubi, would Konoha have been completely destroyed?
If Obito had failed to take control of the Kyuubi, how would he have advanced in his plan?
If Naruto had not been born that night, who would Minato have chosen as the vessel for the Kyuubi?

obito_recruits_pain_and_konan_for_akatsu.name_en: Obito under the name of Madara takes control of the Akatsuki via Nagato
narrative_invariants: Obito exploits Nagato's grief to make him an instrument of his global plan
The Akatsuki that the world knows is a creation of Obito's manipulation, not the original vision of Yahiko
Obito embodies Madara with sufficient conviction to deceive legendary shinobi for decades
This control directly links Nagato's and Konan's fates to Obito's will
alternative_seeds: If Nagato had refused Obito's tutelage after Yahiko's death, would the Akatsuki have existed in this form?
If Obito had revealed his true identity to Nagato, would he have followed him anyway?
If Konan had discovered Obito's manipulation earlier, would she have saved Nagato?

konan_orphan_amegakure_war.name_en: Konan, Yahiko, and Nagato become orphans of the war in Amegakure
narrative_invariants: The civil war in Ame makes Konan, Yahiko, and Nagato orphans
This experience forges the deep desire for peace that unites the trio
The solidarity between the three children forms the emotional foundation of the entire Akatsuki arc
alternative_seeds: If their parents had survived, would Konan have joined Jiraiya as a student?
If Konan had been separated from Yahiko and Nagato at this time, would the peace dream have emerged?
If Amegakure had not been a battlefield, what would have been the trajectory of the trio?

konan_jiraiya_training_origami_jutsu.name_en: Jiraiya trains Konan in ninja techniques, she develops her unique paper style
narrative_invariants: Jiraiya is the only official sensei Konan has ever had
Konan's paper style is entirely self-developed from the foundation laid by Jiraiya
Konan's gratitude toward Jiraiya persists even after her joining Akatsuki
alternative_seeds: If Jiraiya had stayed longer with the trio, would Konan have taken a different path than Akatsuki?
If Konan's talent had been directed toward another element, what combat style would she have developed?
If Konan had accompanied Jiraiya to Konoha, would she have been saved from the cycle of violence earlier?

konan_co_fonde_akatsuki_avec_yahiko.name_en: Konan co-founds the original Akatsuki with Yahiko for the peace of Amegakure
narrative_invariants: The original Akatsuki is a pacifist organization born from the suffering of three orphans
Konan is the only woman among the founding members of the original Akatsuki
Yahiko's vision of peace is also Konan's, who becomes its silent guardian
alternative_seeds: If the original Akatsuki had succeeded in its peace mission, would Hanzo have been overthrown without violence?
If Konan had taken a visible leadership role instead of Yahiko, would the group's dynamics have changed?
If Pain and Konan had not continued the Akatsuki name after Yahiko, would Obito have seized it?

konan_confronts_tobi_obito.name_en: Konan confronts Tobi to protect Nagato's body and the Rinnegan
narrative_invariants: Konan sacrifices her life to try to prevent Tobi from taking the Rinnegan from Nagato
Konan discovers that Tobi has manipulated Nagato by impersonating Madara Uchiha
Konan's death tragically closes the arc of the three orphans of Amegakure
Konan remains loyal until the end to the memory of Yahiko and Nagato
alternative_seeds: If Konan had succeeded in destroying Tobi, would the ninja world war have been avoided?
If Konan had hidden the Rinnegan elsewhere before the confrontation, would the outcome have been different?
If Naruto had arrived in time to Amegakure, would he have saved Konan?
If Konan had revealed Tobi's identity before dying, would that have changed the war's course?

danzo_transplants_sharingan_arm.name_en: Danzō has his Sharingan eyes from Uchiha clan members implanted in his right arm

preconditions.value: suspicion and desire for the clan's abilities

outcomes.value: hides his right arm under bandages

narrative_invariants: Danzō uses unethical methods to increase his personal power in the name of the village's security
The Sharingan eyes implanted come from Uchiha clan members eliminated during the massacre orchestrated with his complicity
Danzō hides this physical modification for decades
alternative_seeds: If Danzō had publicly revealed his Sharingan arm, what impact would it have on his political legitimacy?
If Itachi had refused to cooperate with Danzō, would the massacre have still occurred?
If another Hokage had discovered Danzō's arm before the war, what would have been the consequence for Root?

danzo_acquires_shisui_sharingan.name_en: Danzō steals Shisui Uchiha's eye and implants it in his right orbit

preconditions.value: control the Hokage and village decisions
try to divert the Uchiha coup d'état with genjutsu

narrative_invariants: Danzō is willing to betray and mutilate his own allies to seize a power deemed necessary for Konoha's protection
Stealing Shisui's eye indirectly causes his death and radicalizes Itachi against Danzō
The Kotoamatsukami remains sealed in Danzō's orbit for years as it can only be used once every decade
alternative_seeds: If Shisui had successfully used Kotoamatsukami on the old Uchiha, would the massacre have been avoided?
If Itachi had recovered Shisui's eye immediately after his death, would Danzō have had less power at the Five Kage Summit?
If Kotoamatsukami had been used on Danzō himself, what direction would he have taken?

danzo_appointed_sixth_hokage_candidate.name_en: Danzō is named as a candidate for the position of Sixth Hokage after the Pain's attack on Konoha

preconditions.value: in a coma after treating the Pain's wounded

outcomes.value: candidate for the position of Sixth Hokage
participate in the Five Kage Summit as Konoha's representative

narrative_invariants: Danzō's nomination as Hokage marks the culmination of decades of hidden political ambition
Danzō gains formal power not through recognized merit but through opportunism during the village's institutional weakness
His nomination is viewed negatively by a large portion of Konoha's residents who know his reputation
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had awakened before Danzō's nomination, would he have ever become a candidate?
If Danzō had officially become Sixth Hokage, what policies would he have pursued against Sasuke and Akatsuki?
If Naruto had been present in Konoha during the nomination, would he have opposed it?

yahiko_orphan_war_survival.name_en: Yahiko, Nagato, and Konan survive alone as war orphans
narrative_invariants: The Second Ninja World War strips Yahiko of his family and forces him to survive in the ruins of Amegakure.
The unbreakable bond between Yahiko, Nagato, and Konan arises directly from their shared survival in misery.
Yahiko's hatred of war and desire for peace are rooted in personal total loss.
alternative_seeds: If Yahiko hadn't lost his family, would he have developed the same obsession with peace?
If another ninja had found the trio before Jiraiya, what ideology would have shaped their worldview?
If Amegakure hadn't been a permanent battlefield, would the trio have had the same foundational motivations?

yahiko_jiraiya_ninjutsu_training.name_en: Yahiko learns ninja techniques under Jiraiya's tutelage
narrative_invariants: Jiraiya personally trains Yahiko in ninja techniques for several years before returning to Konoha.
Yahiko retains from Jiraiya not only combat skills but also the ideal that peace is possible.
It was during this training that Yahiko affirmed his will to become a bridge to peace rather than a weapon of war.
alternative_seeds: If Jiraiya had stayed with the trio, would Yahiko have needed to found Akatsuki?
If Yahiko had followed Jiraiya to Konoha, how would that have changed his political trajectory?
If Jiraiya's training had been longer, would Yahiko have survived against Hanzo?

yahiko_akatsuki_croissance_influence.name_en: Yahiko's Akatsuki Gains Influence and Threatens Hanzo's Power
narrative_invariants: The expansion of the Akatsuki under Yahiko constitutes a real threat to Hanzo's authority over Amegakure.
The secret cooperation between Hanzo and Danzo aims to eliminate Yahiko before he becomes uncontrollable.
The popularity of the Akatsuki is paradoxically the direct cause of its founder's death.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzo had decided to negotiate with Yahiko instead of eliminating him, what would the future of Amegakure be?
If Danzo had not supported Hanzo, would the Akatsuki have been able to overthrow Hanzo peacefully?
If Yahiko had been aware of the plot, could he have avoided it without betraying his ideals?

yahiko_rencontre_nagato_konan_initiale.name_en: Yahiko Meets Nagato and Konan and Forms a Survival Group
narrative_invariants: Yahiko naturally assumes leadership of the trio through his courageous character despite his youth.
The meeting of the three orphans is the true starting point of everything that will become the Akatsuki.
The bond between Yahiko and Nagato is that of two complementary souls: one provides vision, the other power.
alternative_seeds: If Yahiko had not taken the role of leader, would Nagato have descended into violence sooner?
If the trio had never formed, would Konan have survived alone?
If Yahiko had met other orphans, would the Akatsuki have a different composition and ideology?

yahiko_hanzo_piege_mort_sacrifice.name_en: Yahiko Sacrifices Himself in the Trap Set by Hanzo to Save Konan

outcomes.value: recovered by Nagato future deva path

narrative_invariants: Yahiko chooses to impale himself on the kunai held by Nagato rather than let Konan die, embodying his ideals of sacrifice for others to the end.
Yahiko's death is the foundational trauma that transforms Nagato into Pain and radicalizes the Akatsuki.
Yahiko remains coherent with his vision until death: he does not yield to violence but chooses sacrifice.
alternative_seeds: If Yahiko had accepted killing Konan to survive, what leader would he have become?
If Nagato had been able to break the chakra threads in time, would they have escaped together?
If Yahiko had survived, would the Akatsuki have remained a pacifist organization and truly brought peace to Amegakure?

rin_joins_team_minato.name_en: Rin Nohara Joins Team Minato with Kakashi and Obito
narrative_invariants: Rin is the medic of the team and the emotional heart of the group.
Rin feels romantic feelings for Kakashi while Obito is in love with her.
The cohesion of Team Minato largely depends on the calming role of Rin.
The emotional triangle Rin-Kakashi-Obito defines the tragic trajectory of the three characters.
alternative_seeds: If Rin had been placed in another team, would Obito have developed the same protective obsession?
If Kakashi had reciprocated Rin's feelings, would the team balance have changed?
If Rin had not been a medic, would the mission to the Land of Snow have failed earlier?

rin_saves_kakashi_eye.name_en: Rin Transplants Obito's Sharingan Eye into Kakashi's Orbit
narrative_invariants: Rin is the physical vector of Obito's legacy to Kakashi, making Kakashi's Sharingan possible.
This surgery accomplished under extreme conditions confirms Rin's exceptional medical abilities.
The eye transfer symbolizes the unspoken promise of protection Rin that Kakashi carries his whole life.
Without Rin, Kakashi's survival and future power would have been compromised.
alternative_seeds: If Rin had not been able to perform the operation, would Kakashi have survived the Third War?
If Obito had survived, would he have accepted that his eye was transplanted into Kakashi?
If the Sharingan had been transplanted into Rin herself, would her trajectory have changed?

rin_captured_kirigakure.name_en: Rin is captured by the ninjas of Kirigakure during the Third Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Rin's capture directly triggers Obito's determination to rescue Kakashi from the mission.
This event highlights the conflict between ninja rules and emotional bonds.
Kiri deliberately exploits Rin's emotional value for strategic plans against Konoha.
Rin's capture accelerates the sequence leading to Obito's apparent sacrifice.
alternative_seeds: If Kakashi had immediately abandoned the mission, would Obito have flown the sacrifice?
If Rin had not been targeted by Kiri, would the Sanbi jinchuuriki plan have been executed differently?
If Rin had escaped alone, would Obito and Kakashi's fates have diverged?

rin_implanted_sanbi_jinchuuriki.name_en: Rin is forcibly implanted as the Sanbi Isobu jinchuuriki by Kirigakure
narrative_invariants: Rin is transformed into an involuntary weapon against her own village, stripping her of agency.
The forced sealing of the Sanbi illustrates the brutality of war and the instrumentalization of individuals.
Rin understands that her only way to protect Konoha is to die before returning.
This act by Kiri directly causes Rin's death and Obito's psychological breakdown toward hatred.
alternative_seeds: If Rin had ignored Kiri's plan, would she have still sacrificed herself upon learning the truth?
If another medic had extracted the Sanbi from Rin, would the tragedy have been avoided?
If Minato had been present during Rin's return, would he have proposed an alternative solution?

rin_self_sacrifice_before_kakashi.name_en: Rin deliberately jumps on Kakashi's Chidori to sacrifice herself and protect Konoha
narrative_invariants: Rin's sacrifice is the foundational act of Obito's transformation into an antagonist.
Rin consciously chooses death to protect her loved ones and village, asserting her values until the end.
Kakashi is condemned to carry the guilt of Rin's death despite his innocence in the choice.
Rin's death under Kakashi's Chidori is the pivotal moment of Obito's emotional arc.
Rin's sacrifice remains an act of protection rather than despair, anchoring her heroic identity.
alternative_seeds: If Obito had reached Rin in time, would he have been able to convince her of another solution?
If Kakashi had canceled his Chidori at the last moment, would Rin have found another way to die?
If Rin had survived and the Sanbi had destroyed Konoha, would Obito have still sought peace?
If Minato had sealed the Sanbi again, would Obito's arc have ever begun?

shisui_awakens_kotoamatsukami.name_en: Shisui awakens the Kotoamatsukami, the supreme genjutsu of the Mangekyou Sharingan
narrative_invariants: The Kotoamatsukami is the most powerful genjutsu known, capable of controlling a target without them realizing it.
Shisui is the only known shinobi to master this technique.
This ability makes Shisui a major strategic asset and a target for those seeking to manipulate the Uchiha clan.
alternative_seeds: If Shisui had used the Kotoamatsukami on Fugaku before the crisis, would the massacre have been avoided?
If Danzo had never known about the Kotoamatsukami, would Shisui have survived?

shisui_itachi_amitie_formation.name_en: Shisui takes Itachi under his wing and becomes his mentor and closest friend
