narrative_invariants: Shisui is the person who most influenced Itachi's values outside of Fugaku.
Their friendship is based on a shared idealism: protecting Konoha without sacrificing the clan if possible.
Shisui's death will be the definitive trigger for Itachi's psychological transformation.
alternative_seeds: If Shisui had survived, would Itachi have refused to carry out the massacre?
If Itachi had not had Shisui as a moral model, would he have chosen a different path?

danzo_vole_oeil_droit_shisui.name_en: Danzo Shimura steals Shisui's right eye to appropriate the Kotoamatsukami
narrative_invariants: The theft of Shisui's eye by Danzo illustrates the extent of corruption within Konoha's institutions.
This act makes Shisui's peaceful plan to stop the Uchiha clan's rebellion impossible.
Danzo will use this eye graft years later against Sasuke, directly linking Shisui's death to the saga of vengeance.
alternative_seeds: If Danzo had not stolen Shisui's eye, would the genjutsu plan on Fugaku have worked and prevented the massacre?
If the Hokage successors had sanctioned Danzo for this act, would the Uchiha clan's history have changed?

shisui_confie_oeil_gauche_itachi.name_en: Shisui entrusts his left eye to Itachi before jumping into the Naka River
narrative_invariants: The bequest of Shisui's left eye to Itachi is an ultimate act of trust and a last hope to protect Konoha.
This eye will be sealed in a crow and used later by Naruto against resurrected Itachi, reversing its effect on Sasuke.
Shisui's suicide in the Naka River is staged to resemble a murder committed by Itachi, accelerating the clan's distrust.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had used Shisui's eye immediately after the massacre, would it have made a difference?
If Shisui had chosen to flee instead of dying, would the clan have found him?

shisui_plan_stopper_rebellion_uchiha.name_en: Shisui develops a secret plan to divert the Uchiha clan's rebellion peacefully through the Kotoamatsukami
narrative_invariants: Shisui is the only Uchiha clan member who attempted a non-violent solution to the political crisis between the clan and Konoha.
His plan involved using the Kotoamatsukami to subtly alter Fugaku's intentions to abandon the coup d'état.
The failure of this plan due to Danzo's sabotage is the direct reason Itachi had no other choice but the massacre.
alternative_seeds: If Shisui's plan had succeeded, would the Uchiha clan have been able to reconcile with Konoha?
If Hiruzen had actively supported Shisui against Danzo, would the crisis have unfolded differently?

mikoto_uchiha_birth.name_en: Birth of Mikoto Uchiha within the Uchiha clan
narrative_invariants: Mikoto is born into the Uchiha clan, inheriting its dojutsu and tensions with Konoha.
Her birth is part of a lineage marked by the curse of the Sharingan and the gradual segregation of the clan.
alternative_seeds: If Mikoto had been born into a different clan, would she have prevented the massacre?
If the Uchiha clan had been better integrated into Konoha, what would have been Mikoto's fate?

mikoto_kushina_friendship.name_en: Deep friendship between Mikoto Uchiha and Kushina Uzumaki

outcomes.value: linked to the Hokage's inner circles via Kushina

narrative_invariants: Mikoto and Kushina are close friends, placing Mikoto within a network of trust near the Hokage.
This friendship transcends the distrust between the Uchiha clan and the village leadership.
Mikoto was one of the few aware of Kushina's pregnancy.
alternative_seeds: If Mikoto had used this friendship to warn Kushina of the imminent conspiracy against the clan, would the massacre have been avoided?
If this friendship had been more publicly known, would it have changed the relationship between the Uchiha clan and Konoha?

mikoto_marriage_fugaku.name_en: Mikoto's Marriage to Fugaku Uchiha, Clan Leader

outcomes.value: household leader of the clan leader

narrative_invariants: Mikoto becomes the wife of the clan leader Uchiha, placing her at the heart of clan political decisions.
This marriage anchors Mikoto in the escalating tensions between the Uchiha clan and the Konoha leadership.
alternative_seeds: If Mikoto had exerted more influence on Fugaku, would she have been able to distance him from the coup plot?
If Mikoto had refused this marriage, what would her role in the clan have been?

mikoto_itachi_birth_motherhood.name_en: Itachi's Birth and the Beginning of Mikoto's Maternal Role
narrative_invariants: Mikoto becomes the mother of Itachi, the child who bears the burden of the Uchiha clan's fate.
Her maternal relationship with Itachi is marked by sincere love, reinforcing the tragedy of the massacre.
alternative_seeds: If Mikoto had known Itachi's intentions before the massacre, would she have tried to dissuade him?
If Mikoto had raised Itachi differently, would the clan/village duty burden have been less destructive for him?

mikoto_death_massacre.name_en: Mikoto Uchiha's Death During the Clan Massacre by Itachi
narrative_invariants: Mikoto accepts her death at Itachi's hands, aware or not of his motivations.
Her death seals the tragic family saga of the Uchiha and establishes the central trauma of Sasuke.
Mikoto's death deprives Sasuke of any maternal figure and familial anchorage.
Mikoto is killed last or among the last victims of the clan, after Fugaku according to canon.
alternative_seeds: If Mikoto had tried to protect Sasuke during the massacre, would Itachi have spared her?
If Mikoto had known Itachi's true motivations, would she have conveyed that message to Sasuke?
If Mikoto had survived, would Sasuke's hatred for Itachi have been as absolute?

nawaki_death_tsunade_necklace.name_en: Nawaki's Death and the Transmission of the Senju Collar to Tsunade
narrative_invariants: Nawaki dreamed of becoming Hokage and received the Senju collar from Tsunade as a good luck charm the day before his death.
Nawaki's death is the first major grief that shatters Tsunade's emotional stability.
The Senju collar becomes a cursed symbol associated with loss for Tsunade.
This loss marks the beginning of Tsunade's legendary misfortune with the collar.
alternative_seeds: If Nawaki hadn't received the collar that day, would he have survived the mission?
If Tsunade had accompanied Nawaki on the mission, would she have been able to save him?
If Nawaki had survived, would Tsunade have still developed her hemophobia?

dan_death_tsunade_abandons_shinobi_life.name_en: Dan Kato's Death and Tsunade's Abandonment of Ninja Life
narrative_invariants: Dan dies in Tsunade's arms despite his medical-nin abilities, which severely traumatizes her.
Dan's death, the second major loss after Nawaki, pushes Tsunade to flee ninja life for years.
Tsunade takes in Shizune, Dan's niece, as an act of responsibility toward his memory.
The Senju collar confirms her cursed reputation with Dan's death.
This flight explains why Tsunade is a wandering ninja at the start of the Sannin arc.
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had saved Dan, would she have stayed in Konoha and become Hokage earlier?
If she hadn't taken Shizune under her wing, who would have been her only link to the ninja world?
If the collar hadn't been tied to these two deaths, would Tsunade have accepted giving it to Naruto?

tsunade_heals_naruto_accepts_hokage.name_en: Tsunade heals Naruto, bets on losing and accepts becoming Hokage
narrative_invariants: Naruto persuades Tsunade with his determination to become Hokage, reigniting her faith in the dreams of her loved ones.
Tsunade voluntarily gives the cursed collar to Naruto, signifying her complete trust in him.
By overcoming her hemophobia during the fight against Orochimaru and Kabuto, Tsunade regains her identity as a ninja.
The refusal to heal Orochimaru is the definitive act of breaking away from her past of fleeing and compromise.
This event is the turning point of Tsunade's return to the ninja world after years of absence.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had lost the bet against Tsunade, would she have still refused Orochimaru?
If Tsunade had healed Orochimaru, what consequences would have occurred for the war and Konoha?
If the collar had killed Naruto like previous bearers, how would Tsunade have reacted?

tsunade_coma_pain_assault.name_en: Tsunade falls into a coma after protecting Konoha from the attack of Pain
narrative_invariants: Tsunade uses all the chakra reserves of the Son Front seal to keep the injured of Konoha alive.
The total exhaustion of her reserves plunges her into a deep coma, rendering her temporarily incapable of governing.
This heroic act exemplifies the absolute sacrifice Tsunade is willing to make to protect her villagers.
Tsunade's coma creates a political vacuum that Danzo exploits to seize power during the Summit of the Five Kage.
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had not awoken during the war, would the Five Kage have maintained their coalition?
If Tsunade had remained incapacitated by the coma, who would have been designated Hokage before Kakashi?
If the coma had lasted longer, who would have led Konoha during the war?

tsunade_reveil_coma_soutien_naruto_hokag.name_en: Awakening from Tsunade's coma and support for Naruto's candidacy as Hokage
narrative_invariants: Tsunade's awakening symbolizes the resilience and will of the Fifth Hokage to not abandon her village even in the worst circumstances.
Tsunade joins the other Kage on the battlefield, affirming the solidarity of the ninja coalition.
Her explicit support for Naruto confirms the symbolic transfer of the Hokage torch she initiated by giving him the collar.
Tsunade's participation in the war alongside the other Kage marks her ultimate commitment as a Hokage in office.
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had not regained consciousness during the war, would the Five Kage have maintained their coalition?
If Tsunade had been permanently incapacitated by the coma, who would have been designated Hokage before Kakashi?
If Tsunade had fought Madara alone, would she have changed the outcome of the battle?

butsuma_leads_senju_warring_states.name_en: Butsuma leads the Senju clan during the era of perpetual wars
narrative_invariants: Butsuma is a pragmatic and merciless war leader who places clan survival above any sentiment.
Under his leadership, the Senju clan faces the Uchiha clan in constant conflicts.
The era of war-clans is marked by constant human losses on both sides.
alternative_seeds: If Butsuma had accepted a truce with the Uchiha earlier, would Konoha have been founded a generation earlier?
If Butsuma had been a less warlike leader, would Hashirama have developed the same aspiration for peace?
If another clan member had led the Senju, would the rivalry with Madara have taken a different form?

butsuma_instructs_hashirama_tobirama.name_en: Butsuma trains his sons Hashirama and Tobirama in the arts of combat and the clan's philosophy of war
narrative_invariants: Butsuma teaches his sons that shinobi must accept death as an inevitable companion.
Butsuma teaches Hashirama and Tobirama that trusting enemies like the Uchiha is a mortal weakness.
Butsuma's war philosophy indirectly shapes Hashirama's pacifist vision in opposition.
alternative_seeds: If Butsuma had shared Hashirama's pacifist vision, would the clan have accepted an alliance with the Uchiha during his lifetime?
If Tobirama had inherited only Butsuma's vision, would he have become an even more hostile leader toward the Uchiha?
If Butsuma had known that Hashirama sympathized with Madara, would he have ended this friendship earlier?

butsuma_discovers_hashirama_friendship_m.name_en: Butsuma discovers the secret friendship between Hashirama and Madara Uchiha and orders their confrontation
narrative_invariants: Butsuma and Tajima Uchiha tend together a trap for their sons at the river's edge.
Butsuma demands without hesitation that Hashirama sacrifice his friendship with Madara for the clan's interests.
This event solidifies the tension between family loyalty and personal ideals that define Hashirama.
The confrontation reveals that Butsuma and Tajima share the same cold war logic despite their enmity.
alternative_seeds: If Butsuma had not discovered his son's friendship with Madara, would Konoha have been founded much earlier without bloodshed?
If Hashirama had obeyed his father and attacked Madara that day, would the ninja world's history have been radically different?
If Butsuma had shown compassion toward his son's friendship, would he have been the architect of peace rather than Hashirama?

butsuma_death_kawarama_itama.name_en: Butsuma's young sons, Kawarama and Itama, die in battle against the enemy clans
narrative_invariants: Butsuma accepts the deaths of his young sons as an inevitable sacrifice of the era of perpetual wars.
The loss of Kawarama and Itama strengthens Hashirama's conviction that his father's war system leads children to death.
This event is one of the deep motivations for Hashirama to found a village where children are protected.
Butsuma represents the generation where war and child-soldier deaths are accepted norms.
alternative_seeds: If Butsuma had refused to send his young sons to battle, would Kawarama and Itama have survived and changed the clan's dynamics?
If Hashirama had not lost his brothers, would he have developed the same urgency to build peace with Madara?
If Butsuma had shown public grief over these losses, would it have changed Hashirama's perception of his father?

neji_branch_seal_trauma.name_en: Neji learns the truth about the branch seal and the death of his father
narrative_invariants: Neji believes his father Hizashi died as a forced sacrifice for the main branch, forging his fatalism.
The cursed branch seal becomes for Neji a symbol of the clan's oppression.
This revelation transforms a prodigy into a bitter ninja convinced that destiny is an immutable cage.
alternative_seeds: If Hiashi had explained the truth to Neji earlier, would the resentment have been avoided?
If Hizashi had survived, what relationship would Neji have had with the main branch?
If the seal didn't exist, would Neji have become the clan's leader?

neji_vs_naruto_chunin_exam_battle.name_en: Neji's battle against Naruto during the Chunin Exam, defeat and revelation about his father
narrative_invariants: Naruto beats Neji by refusing to submit to fatalism, showing that destiny can be changed.
Hiashi reveals to Neji after the battle the truth about Hizashi's voluntary and heroic death, breaking the cycle of hatred.
This is the turning point of Neji's arc, from bitterness to redemption.
Neji understands that his hatred was based on partial truth, and that his father chose his destiny freely.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had lost this battle, would Neji have perpetuated his fatalism indefinitely?
If Hiashi had never revealed the truth, would Neji have eventually joined forces opposed to Konoha?
If Neji had refused to accept the truth about his father, how would it have affected his relationship with Hinata?

neji_becomes_jonin.name_en: Neji Hyuga is promoted to the rank of Jonin, first of his generation's promotion
narrative_invariants: Neji is the first of his generation to reach the rank of Jonin, confirming his status as an absolute prodigy.
His promotion validates that even a member of the secondary branch can excel at the highest level.
This achievement concretely illustrates that destiny is not fixed, directly echoing the lesson learned from Naruto.
alternative_seeds: If Neji had not become Jonin first, would he have been consumed by a new form of resentment?
If the Hyuga clan had refused to recognize his promotion, how would Neji have reacted?
If Naruto had become Jonin before Neji, how would it have affected their dynamic?

neji_rescues_hinata_pain_arc.name_en: Neji protects Hinata during Pain's attack on Konoha
narrative_invariants: Neji actively chooses to protect Hinata and the civilians during Pain's attack, affirming his moral evolution.
His behavior contrasts radically with the Neji at the beginning who hated the main branch.
This event confirms that Neji has fully integrated a code of protection for his kin independently of the caste system.
alternative_seeds: If Neji had been absent from Konoha during Pain's attack, would Hinata have survived?
If Neji had still been plagued by his fatalism, would he have chosen to flee rather than fight?
If Neji had been severely injured during Pain's attack, would his role in the next war have changed?

neji_protects_naruto_hinata_war.name_en: Neji sacrifices his life to protect Naruto and Hinata against Obito's needles during the war
narrative_invariants: Neji dies by choosing freely to sacrifice his life, proving once again that destiny is a choice and not fate.
His death is the emotional catalyst that gives Naruto the strength to continue fighting.
The boy who believed his fate was fixed by his birth in the secondary branch chooses his own end to his story.
Neji's death closes his narrative arc in perfect mirror with his lesson learned during his fight against Naruto in the Chunin Exam.
alternative_seeds: If Neji had survived the war, could he have become the head of the Hyuga clan or reform the branch system?
If Neji had not protected Naruto at this moment, would the war have taken a different turn?
If Hinata had been the one to die in Neji's place, how would it have affected Naruto's arc?
If Neji had still been fatalistic, would he have made the same choice of sacrifice?

haku_parents_death_land_of_water.name_en: Massacre of Haku's parents in the Land of Water

outcomes.value: witness to the murder of his mother and unintentional author of his father's death

narrative_invariants: Kekkei genkai users were persecuted and killed in the Land of Water
Haku instinctively activated his Hyoton to defend himself against his own father
This tragedy conditioned Haku's entire worldview on his own existence
alternative_seeds: If the Land of Water had not persecuted kekkei genkai users, would Haku have grown up normally without ever meeting Zabuza?
If Haku had not activated his kekkei genkai that day, would he have survived without killing his father?
If Haku's mother had hidden her origin from her husband, would the family have survived?

zabuza_recueille_haku.name_en: Zabuza Momochi adopts Haku from the streets of the Land of Water

outcomes.value: defines himself as Zabuza's perfect tool

narrative_invariants: Zabuza saw Haku as a tool rather than a human being, but Haku chose to embrace this role as his reason to live.
The relationship between Haku and Zabuza is one of a deep bond masked under an appearance of utilitarianism.
Haku found in Zabuza the first person who gave him a reason to exist after the death of his parents.
alternative_seeds: If Zabuza had treated Haku as a son rather than a tool, would Haku's fate have changed?
If another ninja had found Haku first, would he have become a ninja of the village rather than a mercenary?
If Haku had refused the role of tool, would Zabuza have still protected him?

haku_combat_sasuke_naruto_pont.name_en: Haku fights Naruto and Sasuke in the Ice Mirror on the Nami no Kuni Bridge

outcomes.value: Kyubi chakra manifested for the first time in combat
chooses not to kill Naruto after seeing the Kyubi
rage and grief facing Sasuke's apparent death
expresses his vision of important people as a source of strength

narrative_invariants: It is in this battle that Naruto's Kyubi chakra manifests for the first time against a mortal threat to his loved ones.
Haku deliberately chooses to spare Naruto despite the order to kill, revealing his internal contradictions.
Sasuke activates the Sharingan in this battle and protects Naruto at the cost of his apparent death.
Haku articulates for the first time to Naruto the philosophy of the ninja who finds his strength in protecting what he cares for.
alternative_seeds: If Haku had killed Naruto and Sasuke on the bridge, would the Team 7 story end there?
If the Kyubi had not manifested, would Haku have been able to defeat Naruto?
If Sasuke had not protected Naruto, would Naruto's rage have still unleashed the Kyubi?

haku_mort_protege_zabuza.name_en: Haku sacrifices himself to protect Zabuza from Kakashi's Chidori

preconditions.value: exist as a tool for Zabuza

outcomes.value: pierced by Kakashi's Chidori while protecting Zabuza
forced to confront his feelings for Haku despite his cold facade
deeply affected by Haku's death and his philosophy
attacks Gato at his own expense after Haku's death

narrative_invariants: Haku's death triggers Zabuza's emotional reaction, leading him to attack Gato, proving that Zabuza loved Haku despite his cold facade
Haku fulfills his role as a tool to the end by dying for Zabuza, but this sacrifice transcends mere utilitarianism
Haku's death is Naruto's first major loss, establishing the series' central theme of important people
Naruto recognizes Haku as a kindred spirit, someone who suffered from loneliness and finds meaning through human connection
alternative_seeds: If Haku had survived, would he have been able to redeem himself and become an ally of Team 7?
If Zabuza had expressed his feelings for Haku before his death, would Haku still have chosen to die for him?
If Kakashi had held back his Chidori upon recognizing Haku as a child, would the sacrifice have been avoided?

haku_edo_tensei_quatrieme_guerre.name_en: Haku is resurrected by the Edo Tensei during the Fourth Great Ninja War

preconditions.value: includes bodies of powerful dead shinobi

outcomes.value: resurrected under edo_tensei under Kabuto's control
also resurrected alongside Haku by edo_tensei
liberated from edo_tensei control after the war by Itachi

narrative_invariants: Haku and Zabuza are resurrected together by the Edo Tensei, symbolizing their unbreakable bond even in death
Haku retains his consciousness and personality under the Edo Tensei but cannot control his actions
The liberation from the Edo Tensei allows Haku to find eternal rest beside Zabuza
alternative_seeds: If Haku had retained control under the Edo Tensei, would he have chosen to fight alongside Naruto?
If Kabuto had not accessed Haku's body, would another kekkei genkai user have filled this role?
If Zabuza and Haku had been resurrected separately, would their reunion have had the same emotional impact?

sarada_awakens_sharingan.name_en: Sarada awakens her Sharingan in seeking the truth about her birth
narrative_invariants: The Sharingan awakens due to an intense emotion tied to family and identity.
Sarada activates her Sharingan without direct instruction from her father, purely out of emotional necessity.
Sarada's identity crisis is the direct catalyst for her first access to the Uchiha kekkei genkai.
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had been present during Sarada's childhood, would her Sharingan have awakened differently?
If Sarada had never discovered the doubt about her motherhood, would she have accessed the Sharingan another way?
If Karin had actually been her biological mother, how would that affect her clan allegiance?

sarada_meets_sasuke_first_reunion.name_en: Sarada meets Sasuke for the first time and confirms her lineage
narrative_invariants: Sasuke recognizes Sarada as his daughter and expresses his love through the protective seal bond.
Naruto plays the role of substitute paternal figure guiding Sarada toward her father.
The meeting with Sasuke resolves Sarada's identity crisis and solidifies her dream of becoming Hokage.
Sarada defines her identity through her emotional bonds rather than biology.
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had rejected Sarada during this meeting, what would become of the Uchiha clan?
If Naruto had not accompanied Sarada, would she have been able to find her father on her own?
If Shin Uchiha had successfully captured Sarada, how would Sasuke have reacted?

shin_uchiha_attack_sarada_sakura.name_en: Shin Uchiha attacks Sarada and Sakura, triggering a family confrontation
narrative_invariants: Sakura is Sarada's true mother, confirmed by DNA and her maternal sacrifice to protect her.
Shin Uchiha represents a perversion of the Uchiha heritage, contrasting with the path chosen by Sarada.
Sarada witnesses Sakura's power and strengthens her admiration for her mother.
The confrontation with Shin definitively resolves the doubt about Sarada's motherhood.
alternative_seeds: If Sakura had been killed by Shin, how would Sarada have built her identity?
If Shin Uchiha had convinced Sarada to join his cause, what would become of the Uchiha clan?
If Orochimaru had not revealed the information about Shin, how would the arc have resolved?

sarada_obtains_mangekyou_sharingan.name_en: Sarada activates the Mangekyou Sharingan after an intense emotional trauma
narrative_invariants: The Mangekyou Sharingan activates inevitably through loss or trauma, perpetuating the curse of the Uchiha clan.
Sarada follows the path of her ancestors despite her desire to break away from the clan's tragedies.
The acquisition of the Mangekyou marks Sarada as an exceptional kunoichi in the new generation.
alternative_seeds: If Sarada had been able to avoid the triggering trauma, would she have sought other paths for progress?
If Sasuke had guided Sarada in using the Mangekyou, would the outcome have been different?
If Sarada obtained the Eternal Mangekyou, who would be her eye donor?

sarada_new_team7_formation.name_en: Formation of the new Team 7 with Boruto, Mitsuki, and Sarada under Konohamaru
narrative_invariants: The new Team 7 deliberately perpetuates the symbolic composition of the original team with heirs of the Uchiha, Uzumaki, and Orochimaru clans.
Konohamaru passes on the legacy of Naruto and the old Team 7 to the next generation.
The rivalry between Sarada and Boruto drives their respective progress toward their goals.
alternative_seeds: If Sarada had been placed in a different team, would she have developed the same ambition to become Hokage?
If Mitsuki had not joined Team 7, how would the group dynamics have evolved?
If Sasuke had been designated as the team's sensei, would that have created family tensions?

orochimaru_sannin_title_granted.name_en: Orochimaru, Jiraiya, and Tsunade receive the title of Legendary Sannin
narrative_invariants: Hanzo of Amegakure grants the title after sparing their lives in exchange for a honorable retirement.
The title of Sannin forever binds the fates of the three characters.
This recognition establishes their status as exceptional shinobi in the eyes of the ninja world.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzo had killed the three instead of sparing them, what would have become of Konoha?
If Orochimaru had never received this title, would he have developed the same grand ambition?
If Tsunade had refused the title, would the trio have maintained its cohesion?

orochimaru_defection_konoha.name_en: Orochimaru is exposed and flees Konoha rather than being judged
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen fails to kill his former student, allowing the threat to grow for years.
Orochimaru's defection symbolizes the failure of the ninja system to contain extreme ambition.
His choice to flee rather than face judgment reveals his tactical clarity.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had had the courage to kill Orochimaru that night, would the invasion of Konoha have occurred?
If Orochimaru had been imprisoned instead of fleeing, would he have reformed his methods earlier?
If Danzo had intercepted Orochimaru instead, would the outcome have been different?

orochimaru_founding_otogakure.name_en: Orochimaru founds Otogakure as a living laboratory for his experiments
narrative_invariants: Otogakure is not a real village but a facade to recruit test subjects.
The founding of Oto allows Orochimaru to build a loyal army and continuous test subjects.
This village embodies the perversion of the ninja village concept serving a single individual.
alternative_seeds: If Otogakure had been a real village with free inhabitants, would Orochimaru have evolved differently?
If other nations had destroyed Oto at its founding, how would Orochimaru have continued his research?
If Kabuto had not joined Oto, would the experiments have been as advanced?

orochimaru_body_transfer_technique_maste.name_en: Orochimaru perfects the Kuchiyose no Jutsu of transferring a soul into a new body
narrative_invariants: The transfer technique is the concrete answer of Orochimaru to the problem of mortality
This mastery directly explains his strategic interest in Sasuke Uchiha's body
The three-year cycle becomes a dramatic structural constraint for Orochimaru's arc
alternative_seeds: If the transfer technique had failed on his first test subject, would Orochimaru have abandoned this path?
If Orochimaru had succeeded in seizing Sasuke's body in time, who would have stopped him?
If the transfer interval had been shorter, would Orochimaru have been more powerful or fragile?

orochimaru_resurrection_after_edo_tensei.name_en: Sasuke resurrects Orochimaru via the Rinne-Sharingan to get answers about the Uchiha
narrative_invariants: Orochimaru's resurrection by Sasuke illustrates the radical reversal of their power dynamic
Orochimaru returns not as the main threat but as a piece of a larger game
This resurrection marks the beginning of Orochimaru's ambiguous redemption arc in the series
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had refused to resurrect Orochimaru, would he have obtained Uchiha information otherwise?
If Orochimaru had exploited his resurrection to betray Sasuke immediately, would the war have had a different outcome?
If Orochimaru had remained dead after Sasuke's attack, would Kabuto have been the sole heir to his knowledge?

shukaku_first_sealing_suna.name_en: Sealing of Shukaku in the first jinchuuriki of Sunagakure
narrative_invariants: Shukaku is used as political weapon by Sunagakure long before Gaara
The sealing marks the beginning of a long series of jinchuuriki for Shukaku
The relationship between Shukaku and its hosts is defined by constraint, not consent
alternative_seeds: If Sunagakure had never sealed Shukaku, how would the village have survived the ninja wars?
If the first jinchuuriki had sympathized with Shukaku, would the beast-host relationship have evolved differently?
If another village had captured Shukaku first, what would be Sunagakure's place in power balance?

shukaku_sealed_in_gaara.name_en: Shukaku sealed in Gaara before his birth by the Fourth Kazekage
narrative_invariants: Shukaku's sealing in Gaara causes the death of his mother Karura and breaks his family
Gaara is condemned from birth to never sleep without risking Shukaku's liberation
Shukaku directly causes Gaara's psychological and social isolation throughout his childhood
Rasa's choice to make his own son a jinchuuriki reveals the cynicism of Sunagakure's ninja politics
alternative_seeds: If Karura had survived, would she have helped Gaara control Shukaku?
If Gaara could sleep normally, would he have developed a different relationship with Shukaku?
If Rasa had sealed Shukaku in another subject than his son, what would be Gaara's fate?

shukaku_vs_kyuubi_suna_invasion.name_en: Shukaku partially released against Gamabunta during the invasion of Konoha
narrative_invariants: This is the first time Shukaku is fully manifested in the main canon
Naruto's victory over Gaara/Shukaku marks Gaara's redemption and path to Kazekage
The battle symbolically opposes two jinchuuriki and their respective beasts
Shukaku displays its true arrogant and sadistic personality during this liberation
alternative_seeds: If Shukaku had been fully released, would Konoha have been destroyed a second time?
If Naruto had not defeated Gaara that day, would Gaara ever become Kazekage?
If Gamabunta had been defeated, which beast would Shukaku have faced next?

shukaku_extraction_by_akatsuki.name_en: Extraction of Shukaku from Gaara by the Akatsuki, temporary death of Gaara
narrative_invariants: The extraction of Shukaku is the first major success of the Akatsuki in their hunt for the tailed beasts.
The death of Gaara forces Naruto to confront the possibility of losing a friend and strengthens his determination against the Akatsuki.
The sacrifice of Chiyo to resurrect Gaara marks a major emotional turning point in the Kazekage Rescue arc.
Shukaku transitions from the state of seal to capture by the Akatsuki for the first time.
alternative_seeds: If the Akatsuki had not succeeded in extracting Shukaku, would Gaara have remained a jinchuuriki during the war?
If Chiyo had not sacrificed her life, how would Gaara have been resurrected?
If Gaara had died definitively, what impact would it have had on the ninja alliance during the Fourth War?

shukaku_alliance_naruto_war.name_en: Shukaku allies with Naruto during the Fourth Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Shukaku accepts to cooperate with Naruto, breaking the tradition of despising humans.
The chakra of Shukaku allows Naruto to access advanced seal abilities including the Magnetic Seal.
The alliance of all tailed beasts with Naruto marks the fulfillment of Hagoromo's vision of coexistence.
Shukaku shows persistent pride while recognizing Naruto's value, a characteristic of its evolution.
alternative_seeds: If Shukaku had refused to ally, would Naruto have reached the Mode Hermite of the Six Paths?
If Shukaku had been sealed in Gaara during the war, how would that have changed alliance tactics?
If Shukaku had remained resentful toward all humans, which tailed beast would have filled that strategic void?

ginkaku_kinkaku_swallowed_kyuubi.name_en: Ginkaku and Kinkaku devoured by the Kyuubi and miraculous survival
narrative_invariants: Ginkaku and Kinkaku survived inside the Kyuubi by devouring its flesh, absorbing its chakra
This survival grants them a chakra partially similar to that of a jinchuuriki
This incident made them infamous as the most notorious criminals of Kumogakure
Their access to the tools of the Sage of Six Paths directly results from this survival
alternative_seeds: If the brothers had not survived the Kyuubi, would they have still obtained the Sage's tools?
If Kumogakure had executed the brothers after capturing the Kyuubi, what consequences would it have for the war?
If other ninjas had attempted the same thing, would they have survived?

ginkaku_kinkaku_acquire_sage_tools.name_en: Ginkaku and Kinkaku seize the five tools of the Sage of Six Paths
narrative_invariants: The five tools of the Sage of Six Paths can only be used by those with sufficient Sage chakra
The chakra absorbed from the Kyuubi allows Ginkaku to wield these legendary artifacts
These tools include the Golden Chalice, the Silver Chalice, the Coral Mirror, the Feather Fan, and the Silk Cord
Possession of these tools makes the brothers a threat comparable to the kage
alternative_seeds: If the brothers had not had the Kyuubi's chakra, would they have been able to wield the Sage's tools?
If these tools had been destroyed before the Fourth War, how would the allied camp have faced the resurgent brothers?
If only one of the brothers had survived, would the other have been able to use all the tools alone?

ginkaku_imprisoned_kumogakure.name_en: Imprisonment of Ginkaku and Kinkaku by Kumogakure after their crimes
narrative_invariants: Despite their crimes, Kumogakure chooses to imprison them rather than execute them due to their extraordinary power
Their imprisonment illustrates Kumogakure's pragmatic policy toward its dangerous military assets
The brothers remain a potential secret weapon of the village even in captivity
alternative_seeds: If Kumogakure had executed the brothers, would they have been resurrected by Kabuto?
If the brothers had escaped prison before the Second War, would the balance of power have changed?
If another village had captured the brothers, would Kumogakure have declared war to retrieve them?

ginkaku_second_great_ninja_war_rampage.name_en: Unleashing of Ginkaku and Kinkaku During the Second Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Kumogakure frees the brothers to use them as weapons during the second world war
The brothers manage to defeat or put in danger the Second Hokage Tobirama Senju
Their involvement in the war demonstrates that even criminals can serve the village's interests in times of crisis
This episode solidifies their legend as the most feared warriors of their era
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had survived the confrontation with the brothers, would the course of the second war have changed?
If Kumogakure had not freed the brothers, would the village have lost the war?
If another kage had faced the brothers, would he have survived?

ginkaku_captured_edo_tensei_fourth_war.name_en: Ginkaku Resurrected in Edo Tensei Captured by the Golden Cage During the Fourth War
narrative_invariants: The resurrected brothers are neutralized through their own tools turned against them
Darui plays a central role in capturing the brothers
The Golden Cage is used to trap Ginkaku after he uttered the sealing word
The brothers' defeat illustrates the narrative irony of falling by their own weapons
alternative_seeds: If the brothers had not been captured by their own tools, how would the Allied Army have neutralized them?
If Darui had not been present, who would have faced the brothers?
If the Sage's tools had been lost before the war, would the resurrected brothers have been even more dangerous?

indra_receives_sharingan_ninshu.name_en: Indra Develops the Sharingan and Masters Hagoromo's Ninshu
narrative_invariants: Indra is the first human to awaken the Sharingan, inheriting the ocular power of Otsutsuki.
His mastery of ninshu surpasses that of Asura in technical and intellectual terms.
Indra transforms Hagoromo's peaceful ninshu into a philosophy of individual force, laying the foundation for ninjutsu.
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had chosen Indra as successor, would ninshu have become a warlike path rather than peaceful?
If Indra had never developed the Sharingan, would the Uchiha kekkei genkai have existed?
If Indra had accepted his father's original philosophy, would the sibling rivalry have disappeared?

hagoromo_choisit_asura_successeur.name_en: Hagoromo Designates Asura as Successor Instead of Indra
narrative_invariants: Hagoromo's choice of Asura breaks Indra and plants the seed of the cycle of hatred between their reincarnations.
Hagoromo prioritized the power of connection and love over pure power, something Indra could never accept.
This rejection is the direct cause of the creation of the Uchiha as a clan doomed to suffer from love turned into hatred.
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had openly explained his reasons to Indra, could the cycle of reincarnation and hatred have been avoided?
If Indra had understood that pure power alone was insufficient, would he have reconciled with Asura?
If Asura had refused the successor mantle, would the rift between the two brothers have been less deep?

indra_attaque_asura_premiere_bataille.name_en: Indra Attacks Asura: First Decisive Fraternal Confrontation
narrative_invariants: Indra awakens the Mangekyou Sharingan by sacrificing his two closest companions, illustrating that the power of the Uchiha arises from loss.
Despite Susanoo and his brute strength, Indra is defeated by Asura supported by his allies, proving Hagoromo's theory.
This battle establishes the pattern that will repeat indefinitely: hatred against love, solitary strength against solidarity.
alternative_seeds: If Indra had not killed his disciples to obtain the Mangekyou, would he have found another way to equalize his power against Asura?
If Indra had defeated Asura in this first confrontation, would the world have been dominated by the philosophy of force?
If his disciples had survived and tried to reason with him, would Indra have turned away from the path of hatred?

indra_influence_black_zetsu_reincarnatio.name_en: Black Zetsu amplifies Indra's hatred to perpetuate the reincarnation cycle
narrative_invariants: Black Zetsu deliberately exploits the fracture between Indra and Asura to direct their reincarnations toward perpetual conflicts serving Kaguya's return.
The Uchiha clan's curse of hatred is not merely a natural tragedy but a conscious manipulation by Black Zetsu.
Indra is both a victim of his own nature and an external manipulation that conditions all his future reincarnations.
alternative_seeds: If Black Zetsu had not manipulated Indra, would the hatred cycle between reincarnations have naturally broken?
If Indra had been aware of Black Zetsu's manipulation, could he have reconciled with Asura during his lifetime?
If Indra's reincarnations had not been directed by Black Zetsu, would Madara have been able to choose peace with Hashirama?

indra_transmet_chakra_sasuke.name_en: Indra transmits his chakra to Sasuke from the spirit world via Hagoromo
narrative_invariants: By transmitting his chakra to his reincarnation Sasuke, Indra inadvertently participates in resolving the cycle he himself initiated.
Sasuke's awakening of the Rinnegan is the culmination of the Indra lineage combined with Hagoromo's will, closing the Otsutsuki heritage.
The hatred cycle initiated by Indra and Asura can only be broken when their reincarnations choose cooperation over destruction.
alternative_seeds: If Indra had refused to transmit his chakra to Sasuke, could Kaguya have been defeated?
If Sasuke had refused Indra's chakra, could he have awakened the Rinnegan through another means?
If Indra's soul had fully accepted his father's vision before transmitting his chakra, would that have changed Sasuke's subsequent behavior?

madara_awakens_rinnegan.name_en: Madara awakens the Rinnegan in his old age
narrative_invariants: Absorbing Hashirama's cells allows Madara to awaken the Rinnegan, fulfilling Hagoromo's prophecy.
Madara is the first human since Hagoromo to possess the Rinnegan while alive.
This awakening confirms that Madara is the reincarnation of Indra Otsutsuki who has achieved his destiny.
The Rinnegan is awakened too late in his natural life to be used directly, forcing Madara to plan his resurrection.
alternative_seeds: If Madara had awakened the Rinnegan earlier, could he have completed the Infinite Tsukuyomi during his lifetime?
If Hashirama had refused to give his cells, would Madara have found another path to the Rinnegan?
If Madara had shared this secret with other Uchiha, would the clan's fate have changed?

madara_entrusts_obito.name_en: Madara entrusts his plan to Obito and designates him as heir
narrative_invariants: Madara uses Izanagi to rewrite his own death and create the illusion of natural death.
Madara chooses Obito as a substitute to execute the Infinite Tsukuyomi in his name.
Madara places his Rinnegan in Nagato without his knowledge to be able to retrieve it during his resurrection.
This event is the pivot that connects the generation of Madara to the Fourth Great Ninja War.
alternative_seeds: If Obito had refused Madara's mentorship, who would carry on the Infinite Tsukuyomi plan?
If Madara had chosen another ninja as heir, would the war's history have changed?
If Nagato had known his eyes belonged to Madara, would he have acted differently?

madara_revived_edo_tensei.name_en: Madara is resurrected by Edo Tensei during the Fourth War
narrative_invariants: Madara's resurrection by Kabuto surpasses Kabuto's own expectations, with Madara restored with more power than anticipated.
Madara fights and dominates the five Kage simultaneously, proving no living ninja can match him alone.
Madara intentionally breaks the constraints of Edo Tensei, asserting his independence even as a resurrected being.
This event materializes the threat Madara represented from the beginning of the series.
alternative_seeds: If Kabuto had not resurrected Madara, would the war have taken a different turn for the allied forces?
If Madara had been effectively controlled by Edo Tensei, would Obito's plan have succeeded?
If the five Kage had coordinated a specific strategy against Madara, could they have contained him?

madara_becomes_juubi_jinchuuriki.name_en: Madara becomes the jinchuuriki of the Juubi and reaches his peak of power
narrative_invariants: By becoming the jinchuuriki of the Juubi, Madara reaches the peak of ninja power as he had planned for decades.
Madara gains the powers of the Six Paths, approaching the level of the Sage of the Six Paths himself.
This event marks the culmination of all the manipulations and sacrifices orchestrated by Madara since his youth.
Even at this stage, Madara is himself manipulated by Black Zetsu without knowing it, illustrating the limits of his control over his fate.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto and Sasuke had been stronger at this moment, could they have prevented Madara from becoming a jinchuuriki?
If Black Zetsu had not manipulated the events, would Madara have truly accomplished the Infinite Tsukuyomi?
If Guy had not opened the eighth gate, how would the confrontation against Madara as a jinchuuriki have unfolded?

madara_absorbs_hashirama_senjutsu_past.name_en: Madara steals Hashirama's cells after the battle of the Valley of the End
narrative_invariants: By appropriating Hashirama's cells, Madara lays the biological foundation necessary for the future awakening of the Rinnegan.
This act shows that even in apparent defeat, Madara had long-term plans with a vision spanning multiple generations.
The fusion of Uchiha and Senju chakra into a single individual replicates the condition of the sons of Hagoromo, allowing access to ancestral powers.
This event retroactively explains why Madara's death at the Valley of the End was a calculated performance.
alternative_seeds: If Madara had not succeeded in stealing Hashirama's cells, would the Tsukuyomi Infinite plan have been impossible?
If Hashirama had discovered the theft of his cells, would he have sought to recover Madara?
If another Uchiha had integrated Hashirama's cells, would he have also awakened the Rinnegan?

kisame_joins_seven_swordsmen.name_en: Kisame joins the Seven Swordsmen of the Mist
narrative_invariants: Kisame obtains Samehada, a living sword that absorbs chakra and chooses its wielders.
His joining the Seven Swordsmen establishes his reputation as an elite shinobi beyond normal bounds.
Kisame is already perceived as an otherworldly being, more like a monster than a man.
alternative_seeds: If Samehada had refused Kisame, would he have been able to reach the S-rank missing-nin status?
If the Seven Swordsmen had remained united, would Kirigakure have resisted the decline of the Water Country?
If Kisame had never joined the Swordsmen, would he have met Itachi?

kisame_kills_cypher_division.name_en: Kisame massacres the cipher division of Kirigakure at Fuguki's order
narrative_invariants: Kisame kills his own comrades to protect secrets, at the order of a traitor he serves without knowing.
This act reveals the fundamental contradiction of Kisame: he seeks absolute truth but serves lies.
It was during this episode that Tobi contacted Kisame and revealed the true nature of Fuguki, triggering his desertion.
alternative_seeds: If Kisame had refused to massacre the cipher division, would he have discovered Fuguki's treachery otherwise?
If Tobi had not manipulated Kisame at this precise moment, would Kisame have remained loyal to Kirigakure?
If Kisame had denounced Fuguki instead of killing him, what impact would that have on Kirigakure's politics?

kisame_itachi_partnership_akatsuki.name_en: Kisame is allied with Itachi Uchiha within the Akatsuki
narrative_invariants: Kisame admires Itachi because he perceives in him a man who has never betrayed his own truth, unlike himself.
Their duo is one of the most feared of the Akatsuki, combining brute power and supreme genjutsu.
Kisame accepts his role as second with rare clarity, comparing himself to a tail without a head.
alternative_seeds: If Kisame had learned Itachi's true motivations earlier, would he have changed his allegiance?
If Kisame had been allied with another Akatsuki member, would his loyalty to the organization have been as absolute?
If Itachi had trusted Kisame with his secrets, what impact would that have on the Uchiha arc?

kisame_suicide_capture_info.name_en: Kisame suicides to prevent the capture of his information by the Alliance shinobi
narrative_invariants: Kisame freely chooses his death to fulfill his mission to the end, refusing any capture.
He calls upon his own sharks and allows himself to be devoured, in an act of absolute coherence with his philosophy.
Might Guy recognizes in him a shinobi of exceptional determination, even as an enemy.
His death transmits Naruto's coordinates to Akatsuki, having a direct impact on the Fourth Great War.
alternative_seeds: If Kisame had been captured alive, what information would he have divulged about Akatsuki and Tobi?
If the scroll had not reached Akatsuki, would the war have taken a different course for Naruto?
If Might Guy had understood the chakra absorption capabilities of Samehada sooner, would the battle have been different?

kisame_infiltre_kumo_samehada_b.name_en: Kisame infiltrates Samehada to spy on Killer B and discover Naruto's location
narrative_invariants: Kisame uses a unique technique of camouflage by blending into his sword Samehada to infiltrate the enemy.
This infiltration shows that Kisame is capable of strategy and patience beyond his reputation for brute force.
The discovery of his camouflage by Might Guy leads to the final battle and Kisame's death.
alternative_seeds: If Kisame's infiltration into Samehada had not been discovered, how long could he have spied on the Alliance?
If Killer B had detected Kisame sooner, would the information transmission to Akatsuki have been avoided?
If Samehada had definitively turned against Kisame to stay with Killer B, would Kisame have abandoned his mission?

itachi_awakens_mangekyou_sharingan.name_en: Itachi awakens the Mangekyou Sharingan after Shisui's death
narrative_invariants: The Mangekyou can only awaken through the pain of losing a loved one.
Itachi chooses to bear the burden of this awakening alone to protect Sasuke.
Each use of the Mangekyou accelerates Itachi's blindness.
alternative_seeds: If Shisui had survived, would Itachi have found another way to prevent the coup d'état?
If Itachi had not awakened the Mangekyou, would he have been able to carry out the clan massacre?
If Sasuke had known from the beginning how to awaken the Mangekyou, would he have sacrificed someone else?

itachi_recrute_par_danzo_anbu.name_en: Itachi joins the ANBU and becomes Danzo's informant within the Uchiha clan
narrative_invariants: Itachi accepts becoming a traitor to his clan to avoid a civil war.
Danzo manipulates Itachi by making him believe the massacre is the only solution.
Itachi's double life officially begins at this moment.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had refused to join the ANBU, would the Uchiha coup d'état have succeeded?
If Hiruzen had found a diplomatic solution, would Itachi have been freed from this role?
If Itachi had denounced Danzo to Hiruzen more explicitly, would the clan's fate have changed?

itachi_rencontre_sasuke_apres_massacre_t.name_en: Itachi finds Sasuke at the hospital and imposes Tsukuyomi to instill the necessary hatred
narrative_invariants: Itachi deliberately plants hatred in Sasuke's heart as a survival motivator.
Tsukuyomi inflicts days of torture on Sasuke in seconds.
Itachi flees after designating Sasuke as his future assassin.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had told the truth to Sasuke that night, would Sasuke have forgiven him?
If Sasuke had not been subjected to Tsukuyomi, would he have had the strength to survive and grow?
If a third party had witnessed this scene, would the truth about the massacre have been revealed sooner?

itachi_confronte_sasuke_konoha_retour.name_en: Itachi and Kisame infiltrate Konoha to track Naruto and confront Sasuke and Kurenai
narrative_invariants: Itachi demonstrates without mercy his absolute superiority over Sasuke to push him to become stronger
This encounter pushes Sasuke indirectly towards Orochimaru
Itachi manipulates every interaction with Sasuke to orchestrate his own future death
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had not humiliated Sasuke in front of his classmates, would Sasuke have still fled to Orochimaru?
If Kisame had captured Naruto during this infiltration, would the entire arc have shifted?
If Sasuke had managed to injure Itachi, would that have changed Itachi's strategy?

itachi_revelation_verite_sasuke_avant_mo.name_en: Itachi removes Orochimaru's implant from Sasuke and reveals the truth about Shisui before dying
narrative_invariants: Even dying, Itachi protects Sasuke from Orochimaru with absolute priority
Itachi carries his secrets into death, leaving Obito to distort the truth
Itachi's last gesture is a touch of face to face, a symbol of their original brotherly bond
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had survived the battle, would he have finally told the truth to Sasuke?
If Sasuke had known before the battle that Itachi was intentionally dying, would he have refused to fight?
If Obito had not revealed the truth in his own way, would Sasuke have become an enemy of Konoha anyway?

chiyo_creates_sasori_parents_puppets.name_en: Chiyo creates puppets in the likeness of Sasori's parents
narrative_invariants: Chiyo creates two puppets representing Sasori's father and mother to try to fill his emotional void.
This well-intentioned act actually deepens Sasori's trauma rather than healing it.
This act irrevocably links Chiyo to Sasori's tragic trajectory.
alternative_seeds: If Chiyo had not created these puppets, would Sasori have developed his body-humans transformation technique?
If Sasori's parents had survived, would Chiyo have dedicated her life to offensive puppetry?

chiyo_poisons_tsunade_ancestor.name_en: Chiyo poisons a member of the Senju family during a confrontation and develops her own antidote
narrative_invariants: Chiyo is recognized as the master of poisons within Suna, capable of bypassing Konoha's antidotes.
Her rivalry with Tsunade is rooted in concrete confrontations during the ninja wars.
This expertise in poison forms the foundation of her identity as a medical ninja.
alternative_seeds: If Tsunade had been able to neutralize Chiyo's poisons earlier, would the balance of power between Konoha and Suna have changed?
If Chiyo had passed her poison secrets to Sasori, would he have been even more dangerous?

chiyo_sasori_battle_and_death.name_en: Chiyo fights Sasori alongside Sakura and sacrifices herself to revive Gaara
narrative_invariants: Chiyo uses the life transfer technique, giving her own existence to bring Gaara back to life.
Her battle against Sasori represents the ultimate confrontation between the creator and his cursed creation.
Chiyo's death is an act of conscious redemption, she assumes responsibility for what Sasori has become.
Sakura plays a crucial role in defeating Sasori thanks to Chiyo's quick teachings.
Chiyo recognizes in dying that she could have been a better grandmother to Sasori.
alternative_seeds: If Chiyo had refused to sacrifice herself, would Gaara have remained dead and would Suna have lost its Kazekage?
If Chiyo had passed the life transfer technique to someone before dying, what impact would that have had on the ninja war?
If Sasori had survived, would he have been redeemed by Chiyo as she secretly hoped?

chiyo_trains_sasori_puppeteer.name_en: Chiyo trains Sasori in puppeteering from childhood
narrative_invariants: Chiyo is the direct source of Sasori's puppeteer genius, teaching him everything she knows.
Sasori eventually surpasses his master and develops techniques that Chiyo would never have imagined.
The transmission of this art becomes the strongest and most tragic bond between grandmother and grandson.
alternative_seeds: If Chiyo had refused to teach puppeteering to Sasori, would he have found another path for his talent?
If Chiyo had had other students, the puppet techniques of Suna would have diverged differently?

chiyo_joins_rescue_gaara_team.name_en: Chiyo joins the rescue team of Konoha to rescue Gaara kidnapped by Akatsuki
narrative_invariants: Chiyo overcomes her distrust of Konoha to participate in Gaara's rescue.
Her meeting with Naruto and her determination remind her of the possibility of a better world.
This mission is the final act of her ninja career and her legacy to Suna's future.
alternative_seeds: If Chiyo had refused to join the team, would the outcome of the battle against Sasori have been different?
If Chiyo's distrust of Konoha had prevailed, what consequences would that have had for relations between the two villages?

jiraiya_prophecy_great_student.name_en: The Sage of the Swamp predicts to Jiraiya that he will train the child of the prophecy
narrative_invariants: The prophecy of the Sages of the Swamp directs Jiraiya's entire life toward seeking and training the Chosen.
Jiraiya initially believes Nagato is the child of the prophecy before reconsidering in favor of Naruto.
Mount Myoboku is the sacred place where Jiraiya maintains a special connection with the Sages of the Swamp.
alternative_seeds: If Jiraiya had never received this prophecy, would he have taken Naruto as a student?
If Nagato had kept his promise of peace, would the prophecy have been fulfilled differently?
If the prophecy had designated another child, would Jiraiya have abandoned Naruto?

jiraiya_trains_sannin_hiruzen.name_en: Hiruzen Sarutobi entrusts the young Jiraiya, Tsunade, and Orochimaru to his tutelage
narrative_invariants: The three future Sannins are trained together under the direct tutelage of the Third Hokage.
The bonds formed during this training influence the divergent paths of the three students for the rest of their lives.
Jiraiya displays raw but inconsistent talent from childhood, contrasting with Orochimaru's early genius.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had separated the three students, would the Sannins have still reached their power level?
If Jiraiya had been closer to Orochimaru, would he have been able to prevent his fall?

jiraiya_sannin_title_rain_country.name_en: Jiraiya, Tsunade, and Orochimaru receive the title of Sannins during the Rain Country battle
narrative_invariants: The title of Sannin is granted by Hanzo of Ame in recognition of their survival during a lost battle.
This battle marks the transition of the three ninjas from the status of war soldiers to that of living legends.
Jiraiya meets the orphans of the Rain Country shortly after this battle, leading him to train Nagato, Yahiko, and Konan.
alternative_seeds: If Hanzo had killed the three ninjas instead of saving them, would the history of the Sannins ever have existed?
If Jiraiya had not met the orphans shortly after this battle, would Nagato have developed the Rinnegan without a mentor?

jiraiya_trains_nagato_rinnegan.name_en: Jiraiya teaches ninja techniques to Nagato and discovers the Rinnegan of the orphan
narrative_invariants: Jiraiya spends several years personally training Nagato, Yahiko, and Konan, preparing them to protect the oppressed.
The discovery of Nagato's Rinnegan temporarily convinces Jiraiya he has found the Prophet's Child.
This teaching creates the emotional bond that makes Jiraiya's death against Pain (Nagato) tragically irreconcilable.
alternative_seeds: If Jiraiya had continued mentoring the trio until adulthood, would Yahiko have died and Pain have been born?
If Nagato had not had the Rinnegan, would Jiraiya have still trained him as long?
If Jiraiya had publicly revealed Nagato's Rinnegan, how would Konoha have reacted?

jiraiya_infiltrates_ame_investigates_pai.name_en: Jiraiya infiltrates the Rain Country to investigate the Akatsuki and the identity of Pain
narrative_invariants: Jiraiya faces alone the six bodies of Pain and discovers at the cost of his life that his student Nagato has become the leader of the Akatsuki.
His final act is to encode a vital message on the back of Fukasaku, extending his influence beyond his death.
Jiraiya's death against his own student Nagato is the tragic conclusion of the prophecy and his choices as a mentor.

sasuke_awakens_sharingan.name_en: Sasuke awakens the Sharingan during the mission in the Wave Country
narrative_invariants: The Sharingan of Sasuke awakens under emotional pressure and the danger of death, confirming the Uchiha heritage.
The battle against Haku reveals the deep nature of the bond between Sasuke and Naruto.
Sasuke is ready to die for Naruto, contradicting his solitary mission of vengeance.
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had not awakened the Sharingan against Haku, would he have survived the Wave Country?
If Sasuke had seen Naruto die here, would his vengeance arc have been altered?
If Naruto had not awakened the Kyuubi's chakra at the same time, what would have been the outcome of the battle?

sasuke_retrieval_failure_sound_four.name_en: Sasuke escapes the retrieval team and crosses the Valley of the End
narrative_invariants: Sasuke's defection is consummated after defeating Naruto, marking the definitive break with Konoha.
Sasuke consciously chooses power over friendship, defining the trajectory of his arc for years.
The retrieval team fails despite their efforts, highlighting that Sasuke cannot be saved by force.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had successfully brought Sasuke back by force, would the vengeance cycle have been broken sooner?
If Sasuke had been more severely injured by the team, would he have still joined Orochimaru?
If Shikamaru had been able to intercept Sasuke alone, would persuasion have worked?

sasuke_learns_itachi_truth_from_madara.name_en: Tobi reveals to Sasuke the truth about the Uchiha clan massacre by Itachi
narrative_invariants: The revelation transforms Itachi from monster to martyr, forcing Sasuke to completely rebuild his sense of self.
Sasuke turns his hatred toward Konoha and its leaders rather than finding peace.
This scene establishes that the system of Konoha, not a single person, is the source of the Uchiha tragedy.
alternative_seeds: If Tobi had never revealed the truth to Sasuke, would he have returned to Konoha after killing Itachi?
If Sasuke had learned the truth directly from Itachi, would his reaction have been different?
If Sasuke had refused to believe Tobi, could the global ninja war have been avoided?

sasuke_edo_tensei_itachi_encounter.name_en: Sasuke fights and reconciles with Itachi, who was resurrected by Edo Tensei during the Fourth War
narrative_invariants: Itachi expresses his brotherly love to Sasuke directly for the first and last time, closing their relationship.
Itachi refuses to change Sasuke by force, accepting his choices, unlike all his previous manipulations.
Itachi's definitive death leaves Sasuke without a main enemy, accelerating his ideological transformation towards revolution.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had survived long enough to talk to Sasuke about Naruto, would Sasuke's turnabout have happened earlier?
If Sasuke had fought Kabuto directly without Itachi, would he have discovered the truth on his own?
If Itachi had chosen to erase Sasuke's memories one last time, what Sasuke would have become?

sasuke_receives_hagoromo_power_revolutio.name_en: Hagoromo Otsutsuki grants his chakra to Sasuke and he announces his revolution of the ninja world
narrative_invariants: Sasuke receives the highest power of ninja history but uses it to announce a personal revolution rather than redemption.
Sasuke's choice reveals that even Hagoromo's wisdom isn't enough to correct an ideology forged by suffering.
The final confrontation between Naruto and Sasuke is made inevitable by this event.
alternative_seeds: If Hagoromo had refused to give his chakra to Sasuke due to his intentions, would the battle against Kaguya have been won?
If Sasuke had announced wanting to protect the world rather than revolutionize it, would Naruto have followed?
If Sasuke had killed the Five Kage as planned, what would the ninja world look like post-war?

hiruzen_becomes_third_hokage.name_en: Hiruzen Sarutobi becomes the Third Hokage
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen is designated Hokage by Tobirama himself before his death during the First Great War.
Hiruzen inherits a village forged in the conflicts of the Senju and Uchiha and must ensure its stability.
The title of Third Hokage embodies the continuity of the Fire of Will philosophy.
alternative_seeds: If Tobirama had survived, would Hiruzen have ever become Hokage?
If Hiruzen had refused the title, who would have led Konoha during this critical period?
If another candidate had been designated, would the policy towards the Uchiha clan have been different?

hiruzen_forme_les_trois_legendaires_sann.name_en: Hiruzen Sarutobi forms and trains Jiraiya, Tsunade, and Orochimaru
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen immediately recognized the exceptional potential of his three students and transmitted all of his knowledge to them.
The master-student relationship between Hiruzen and the Sannin conditions the entire trajectory of Jiraiya, Tsunade, and Orochimaru.
Despite his kindness, Hiruzen was unable to prevent Orochimaru's descent into forbidden arts.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had detected Orochimaru's illicit experiences earlier, would he have been able to save him from corruption?
If the Sannin had not been formed by Hiruzen, would Konoha have had such a striking offensive capability?
If Hiruzen had refused to train Orochimaru, would the threat he represented have been less?

hiruzen_bannit_orochimaru_de_konoha.name_en: Hiruzen confronts Orochimaru and lets him flee Konoha
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen was unable to deliver the fatal blow to his former student due to affection, a regret he would carry until his death.
Orochimaru's escape represents Hiruzen's most personal and moral failure as Hokage.
This incident highlights the fundamental tension between a master's love for his student and the responsibilities of a village leader.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had eliminated Orochimaru during this confrontation, would the invasion of Konoha have occurred?
If Orochimaru had not fled, would the succession to the Hokage position have been different?
If Hiruzen had chosen Orochimaru as his successor, would his criminal trajectory have been avoided?

hiruzen_reprend_titre_hokage_apres_minat.name_en: Hiruzen takes the title of Third Hokage after Minato's death
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen leaves his retirement to take charge of Konoha during a crisis after Minato's death.
It is Hiruzen who decides to conceal the identity of Naruto's parents to protect him from retaliation.
This return to power extends the era of Sarutobi and indefinitely delays the emergence of a natural successor.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had revealed the identity of Naruto's parents, would his life have been different?
If another jonin had assumed provisional leadership, would the policy toward the Uchiha clan have changed?
If Hiruzen had officially adopted Naruto, would his fate have been different?

hiruzen_mort_combat_orochimaru.name_en: Hiruzen Sarutobi fights Orochimaru and dies sealing his soul
narrative_invariants: Hiruzen sacrifices his life to protect Konoha by using the technique of the Death God to seal Orochimaru's soul.
His death marks the end of the era of the Hokage founders and forces Konoha to find a new leader during a period of instability.
This final act confirms that Hiruzen has always placed the well-being of his village above his own survival.
Hiruzen's sacrifice deprives Orochimaru of his snake jutsu and irrevocably changes the balance of power between Konoha and Otogakure.
alternative_seeds: If Hiruzen had completely sealed Orochimaru's soul, would the threat of Otogakure have persisted?
If Hiruzen had survived, would he have willingly ceded the Hokage title to Tsunade?
If Hiruzen had refused to sacrifice his life, would Konoha have resisted the Suna-Otogakure invasion?
If the Shinigami had not been summoned, would Hashirama and Tobirama have been sealed differently?

mei_becomes_mizukage.name_en: Mei Terumi becomes the Fifth Mizukage of Kirigakure
narrative_invariants: The end of Yagura's bloody reign opens the way for Mei to rebuild Kiri from within.
Mei embodies the transition from the dark period of the blood-slicked village of mist to an era of reforms.
Her ascension to the position of Mizukage is the direct result of her resistance to the previous regime.
alternative_seeds: If Yagura had survived and retained power, would Mei have led an open revolution against him?
If Mei had not taken the lead of Kiri, would the village have remained diplomatically isolated?
If Yagura's manipulation by Obito had been revealed earlier, would the power transition have been different?

mei_five_kage_summit_defense.name_en: Mei Terumi fights Sasuke at the Summit of the Five Kage

outcomes.value: revelees en combat : Futton et Yoton
pousse a ses limites par les Kage

narrative_invariants: Mei demonstrates that she possesses two rare kekkei genkai, Futton and Yoton, making her one of the most powerful ninja in the world.
Her battle against Sasuke at the summit establishes her credibility as a military leader, not just a diplomat.
The Five Kage gathered represent the first coalition of villages against the Akatsuki and Uchiha threats.
alternative_seeds: If Mei had neutralized Sasuke at the summit, would the Fourth Great Ninja War have taken a different course?
If Mei had negotiated with Sasuke rather than fighting him, would an alliance have been possible?
If her two kekkei genkai had been known to Obito before the summit, would he have adapted his plan accordingly?

mei_fourth_war_alliance_forces.name_en: Mei Terumi fights within the Shinobi Alliance during the Fourth Great Ninja War

outcomes.value: renforcee par la presence des Kage

narrative_invariants: Mei's participation in the war confirms Kiri's integration into the international community of ninja villages.
The Kage fight on the front lines, symbolizing their direct involvement beyond the role of command.
The Fourth Great Ninja War is the first conflict in which Kiri fights alongside Konoha rather than against it.
alternative_seeds: If Kiri had refused to join the Shinobi Alliance, would the outcome of the war have been different?
If Mei had been captured or killed during the war, who would have led Kiri and maintained the alliance?
If Mei had chosen to remain neutral as a political option, would that have weakened the Alliance?

mei_mizukage_reforms_kiri.name_en: Mei Terumi initiates the internal reforms of Kirigakure and abolishes the persecution of kekkei genkai
narrative_invariants: The persecution of kekkei genkai carriers under Yagura was one of the darkest marks of the village of blood mist.
Mei, herself a carrier of two kekkei genkai, symbolizes the reversal of this discriminatory policy.
The reforms of Kiri under Mei are a prerequisite for her participation in the Summit of the Five Kage and the Ninja Alliance.
alternative_seeds: If Mei had not abolished the persecution of kekkei genkai, would Suigetsu and other rare-ninja talents have fled or betrayed Kiri?
If the reforms had failed against internal conservative factions, would a civil war have erupted in Kiri?
If the persecution policy had persisted, would Kiri have been excluded from the Summit of the Five Kage?

isshiki_betrayed_by_kaguya_earth.name_en: Kaguya betrays Isshiki and leaves him to die on Earth

outcomes.value: survival and revenge plan to cultivate new fruit

narrative_invariants: Kaguya's betrayal is the foundational moment that explains Isshiki's entire trajectory on Earth.
Isshiki would have been sacrificed as fertilizer for the Divine Tree by Kaguya, revealing the cruel hierarchy of the Otsutsuki clan.
This betrayal forces Isshiki to adopt an entirely new parasitic survival strategy for the clan.
The failure of the original plan by two Otsutsuki becomes the driver of the entire Boruto arc.
alternative_seeds: If Kaguya had not betrayed Isshiki, would the Divine Tree have been planted since antiquity, eliminating all human chakra?
If Isshiki had survived with his body intact, would he have fought Kaguya for the fruit?
If Isshiki had chosen another Otsutsuki partner, would the betrayal have been avoided?

isshiki_possesses_jigen_monk.name_en: Isshiki takes possession of the body of monk Jigen and engraves a Kama
narrative_invariants: Jigen is an incompatible host: he cannot fully contain Isshiki's powers without dying quickly.
The Kama engraved on Jigen is both a survival tool for Isshiki and a prison for his host.
This possession demonstrates Isshiki's unique ability to miniaturize his body to infiltrate a host.
The act establishes the precedent of the Kama as a mechanism for resurrection and genetic data transfer of the Otsutsuki.
alternative_seeds: If Jigen had been a high-ranking ninja instead of a monk, would his body have been able to contain Isshiki fully?
If Isshiki had been able to engrave his Kama on multiple humans simultaneously, would he have abandoned the search for Kawaki?
If Jigen had resisted the possession, would Isshiki's story on Earth have ended at this stage?

isshiki_jigen_combat_naruto_sasuke.name_en: Jigen-Isshiki fights and defeats Naruto and Sasuke, imprisoning Naruto
narrative_invariants: This is the first time Naruto and Sasuke are simultaneously defeated in such a convincing manner by a single opponent.
Isshiki's victory establishes his status as a threat surpassing anything the ninja world has faced after Kaguya.
Naruto's imprisonment deprives Konoha of its main strength and forces Boruto and Kawaki to act.
The battle irreversibly damages Jigen's body, hastening the urgent need for a new vessel.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto and Sasuke had coordinated a different strategy, would they have prevented the imprisonment?
If Isshiki had not used Jigen's body in this battle, how long would he have been able to maintain this form?
If Boruto had been present during this battle, would the Karma of Momoshiki have changed the outcome?

isshiki_resurrection_jigen_final_form.name_en: Isshiki fully resurrects in the body of dying Jigen and fights Naruto in Baryon mode

outcomes.value: in mortal danger in Baryon mode

narrative_invariants: Naruto's Baryon mode is the existential response to a threat deemed impossible to defeat otherwise.
Isshiki's resurrection without a valid vessel is his implicit death sentence.
Isshiki engraves a Kama on Boruto as a last resort, planting the seed for the future arc with Code and Eida.
The death of Isshiki without a valid Otsutsuki successor is a unique rupture in the Otsutsuki clan cycle.
alternative_seeds: If Kawaki had not erased his Kama, would Isshiki have survived by obtaining a better vessel?
If Naruto had not used the Baryon mode, was there another way to slow down Isshiki?
If Isshiki had successfully engraved a viable Kama on Boruto before dying, would the Otsutsuki plan have continued normally?
If Code had been present during the resurrection, would Isshiki have entrusted the Kama to Boruto instead?

gaara_born_jinchuuriki_shukaku.name_en: Gaara is sealed as a jinchuuriki of the Shukaku before his birth
narrative_invariants: The sealing of the Shukaku in Gaara before his birth is the origin of all his suffering and isolation
The death of his mother Karura during childbirth deprived him from the start of a fundamental emotional bond
Rasa instrumentalizes his own son as a military tool, establishing a father-son relationship based on fear and control
alternative_seeds: If Karura had survived, would Gaara have developed a less destructive identity despite the Shukaku?
If another candidate had been chosen as a container, would Gaara have become a regular ninja of Suna?
If Rasa had abandoned this plan, would Suna have found another way to strengthen its military power?

gaara_yashamaru_verite_traumatisme.name_en: Yashamaru reveals the truth and attempts to assassinate Gaara at Rasa's order

outcomes.value: dead by his own jutsu or killed by Gaara
exist only for himself and kill
graves the kanji for love on his forehead
absolute hatred and distrust of his father

narrative_invariants: Yashamaru's betrayal, the only person Gaara trusted, crystallizes his worldview as a total hostile place
Gaara tattoos the kanji for love on his forehead as a nihilistic affirmation of his exclusive love for himself
This event is the turning point that transforms a wounded child into a threat for Suna
alternative_seeds: If Yashamaru had refused Rasa's order, would Gaara have developed a healthy affectional bond?
If Gaara had later learned his mother Karura truly loved him, would that have changed his trajectory before Naruto?
If Rasa had chosen another method to control Gaara, would the psychological breakdown have been avoided?

gaara_vs_rock_lee_examen_chunin.name_en: Gaara faces Rock Lee during the Chunin Exam preliminaries and breaks his leg

preconditions.value: seeks to kill to prove his existence

outcomes.value: broken leg and arm crushed by Gaara's sand
confirmed as a mortal threat to Konoha's genins
temporarily pierced by Lee's weights before Gaara's final victory

narrative_invariants: The battle against Rock Lee is the first time Gaara's sand shield is pierced, sowing the seed of doubt about his invulnerability
Gaara deliberately breaks Lee's leg after his victory, revealing his calculated cruelty and lack of empathy at this stage
This confrontation establishes the contrast between two shinobi who have both suffered and chosen opposing paths
alternative_seeds: If Rock Lee had defeated Gaara, what would have been the impact on Gaara's already fragile psychology?
If Gaara had spared Lee without external intervention, would that have signaled an early inner evolution?
If Lee's weight technique had been sufficient to completely break the sand, how would Gaara have reacted to his first defeat?

gaara_becomes_godaime_kazekage.name_en: Gaara is named the Fifth Kazekage of Sunagakure

preconditions.value: redemption in progress after defeat by Naruto
Rasa died during the invasion of Konoha
previously feared, on the path to reconciliation

outcomes.value: protection of Sunagakure and its inhabitants
transition from outcast to respected leader
prove his worth through protection rather than destruction

narrative_invariants: Gaara's nomination as Kazekage represents the culmination of his redemption arc begun by his defeat against Naruto
Gaara now leads the village that once feared and wanted to eliminate him, completely inverting his relationship with Suna
His ascension to the rank of Kazekage proves that a rejected jinchuuriki can become a pillar of his community
alternative_seeds: If the elders of Suna had refused to name Gaara Kazekage, would he have found another form of belonging?
If Gaara had inherited the title before his encounter with Naruto, would he have led Suna as a force of destruction?
If Temari or Kankuro had been chosen instead, what would Gaara's path have been without this responsibility?

gaara_discours_alliance_ninja_guerre_qua.name_en: Gaara delivers the unifying speech before the Alliance of Shinobi before the war against Madara

preconditions.value: jinchuuriki who has experienced hatred and redemption

outcomes.value: commander of the surprise division of the Alliance
Gaara's words remind him of his own path

narrative_invariants: Gaara embodies the most radical transformation of the series, from solitary monster to unifying voice of the ninja nations
His personal testimony on hatred and redemption gives unique legitimacy to his calls for unity
This speech symbolically closes his arc by making him the completed mirror of what Naruto seeks to become for all
alternative_seeds: If Gaara had not been in position as Kazekage, would another leader have unified the Alliance with as much emotional legitimacy?
If Naruto had not defeated Gaara years prior, would Gaara have had the personal history necessary to convince the nations?
