If the Alliance had failed to form, how would Gaara have chosen his side in a more fragmented conflict?

zabuza_kills_graduating_class.name_en: Zabuza massacre the graduating class of Kirigakure Academy
narrative_invariants: Zabuza, before even becoming an official ninja, kills the entire graduating class of Kirigakure Academy.
This act earns him the title of Demon of the Hidden Mist and shapes his identity as a ruthless killer.
This event directly leads to the questioning of the tradition of deadly duel graduation in Kirigakure.
alternative_seeds: If Zabuza had not massacred his class, would he have become a ninja through a conventional path?
If the tradition of deadly duel graduation had not existed, would Zabuza have become as feared a criminal?
If a teacher had stopped him, would the history of the Seven Ninja Swordsmen of the Mist have been different?

zabuza_joins_seven_swordsmen.name_en: Zabuza joins the Seven Ninja Swordsmen of the Mist and acquires Kubikiribochi
narrative_invariants: Zabuza becomes one of the most feared members of the Seven Ninja Swordsmen of the Mist, an elite group of Kirigakure.
He acquires the Kubikiribochi, the great decapitating scythe, which becomes his signature weapon in battle.
His membership in this group confirms his status among the most dangerous shinobi in the Water Country.
alternative_seeds: If Zabuza had not obtained the Kubikiribochi, would he have developed a completely different combat style?
If the Seven Ninja Swordsmen of the Mist had rejected Zabuza, would he have tried to eliminate them?
If Zabuza had remained loyal to Kirigakure after his failure, would he have reformed the inner village?

zabuza_tentative_coup_kirigakure.name_en: Zabuza attempts a coup d'état against the Mizukage and becomes a missing-nin
narrative_invariants: Zabuza attempts to assassinate Yagura, the fourth Mizukage, and fails, forcing him to flee Kirigakure.
This failure makes him a missing-nin hunted and marks the definitive break between him and his native village.
His attempted coup reveals an political ambition beyond the simple role of mercenary or killer.
alternative_seeds: If Zabuza had succeeded in killing Yagura, would he have reformed Kirigakure according to his vision?
If Obito had not manipulated Yagura, would Zabuza's coup have succeeded?
If Zabuza had remained in Kirigakure after his failure, would he have been executed or imprisoned?

zabuza_adopte_haku.name_en: Zabuza adopts Haku and makes him his tool and combat partner
narrative_invariants: Zabuza adopts Haku, the child bearer of the Hyoton kekkei genkai, not out of compassion but to make him a weapon.
Haku develops an absolute devotion to Zabuza, ready to sacrifice his life for him.
This ambiguous relationship between tool and affection is the emotional core of the Water Country arc.
alternative_seeds: If Zabuza had treated Haku as a son rather than a tool, would their destinies have been different?
If Haku had not had the kekkei genkai, would Zabuza have adopted him anyway?
If another missing-nin had found Haku before Zabuza, would the Water Country arc have occurred?

zabuza_mort_sacrifice_pays_vagues.name_en: Zabuza kills Gato and dies alongside Haku in the Water Country
narrative_invariants: Zabuza, touched by Naruto's words on bonds, recovers his humanity and avenges Haku's death by killing Gato despite his injuries.
In dying, Zabuza asks to be placed beside Haku, revealing that beneath the demon's mask lay a man capable of attachment.
The death of Zabuza and Haku marks Naruto's first major lesson on suffering, bonds, and the definition of a ninja.
This event marks the end of the series' foundational arc and establishes Naruto's central philosophy on redemption.
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had not made Zabuza cry with his words, would Zabuza have served Gato to the end?
If Haku had not died, would Zabuza have been able to envision a different life as a missing-nin?
If Zabuza had survived, would he have become an ally of Konoha or a rival of Kisame for the Kubikiribochi?
If Kakashi had killed Zabuza during their first encounter, would the emotional arc of Naruto have developed?

boruto_chunin_exam_cheating_disqualifica.name_en: Boruto Cheats in the Chunin Exam and is Disqualified by Naruto
narrative_invariants: Boruto refuses the path of legitimacy and chooses the shortcut, reflecting his fear of not being up to his father's standards
Naruto publicly disavows his son, marking the most explicit rupture between them
Cheating is the direct catalyst for Momoshiki's attack and thus the acquisition of the Karma
alternative_seeds: If Boruto hadn't cheated, would Momoshiki have found another opportunity to mark Boruto?
If Naruto hadn't disqualified Boruto, would their relationship have evolved differently?
If Sasuke had advocated for Boruto, would Naruto's judgment have changed?

boruto_kills_momoshiki_first_karma.name_en: Boruto and Naruto Defeat Momoshiki Otsutsuki, Boruto Receives the Karma
narrative_invariants: Momoshiki's death is simultaneously the beginning of Boruto's gradual possession
The Karma transforms Boruto from an ordinary ninja into a cosmic stake tied to the Otsutsuki
The victory of father and son together symbolizes reconciliation before the tragic separation to come
alternative_seeds: If Boruto hadn't delivered the final blow, would another character have received the Karma?
If Naruto had been freed without Boruto's help, would the father-son dynamic have evolved differently?
If the Karma hadn't been passed on, what would Momoshiki's contingency plan have been?

boruto_karma_awakening_against_boro.name_en: Boruto's Karma Awakens Against Boro to Save Naruto

preconditions.value: imprisoned in a Kama seal by Jigen

outcomes.value: first episode of complete possession by Momoshiki

narrative_invariants: The first complete possession of Boruto by Momoshiki reveals that the Karma is an existential threat and not just a combat advantage
Boruto is capable of saving his father but at the cost of losing control of himself, the central theme of his arc
The team gathered around Kawaki and Boruto acts without adult supervision, marking their autonomy
alternative_seeds: If Momoshiki hadn't taken control, would the team have defeated Boro another way?
If Kawaki had used his own Karma instead, what would have happened?
If Naruto had watched the possession, would his view of Boruto's Karma have changed?

boruto_isshiki_fight_naruto_sasuke.name_en: Boruto Manifests Momoshiki Against Isshiki, Sasuke Loses His Rinnegan

outcomes.value: stops the possession by cutting Boruto's arm

narrative_invariants: The destruction of Sasuke's Rinnegan by Boruto possessed is an irreversible loss that redraws the power of the previous generation
Boruto is simultaneously a savior and a threat to his allies, a contradiction that defines his identity
Kawaki's intervention to stop the possession signifies their ambiguous brotherly bond
alternative_seeds: If Sasuke had kept his Rinnegan, would the following arc's trajectory have been different?
If Kawaki hadn't pulled Boruto's arm, would Momoshiki have killed Naruto?
If the Baryon Mode hadn't been used, would Isshiki have succeeded in reincarnating in Kawaki?

boruto_code_framing_defection.name_en: Boruto is Accused of His Parents' Murder, Framing of Code, and Leaves Konoha with Kawaki

outcomes.value: altered by Kawaki via Otsutsuki power
sealed or apparently dead according to canon interpretation
prove his innocence and find his life

narrative_invariants: The betrayal of collective memories places Boruto in an absolute outcast position, echo of Itachi's sacrifice of his reputation
Boruto loses his village, his family, and his social identity, representing the total fall of a Naruto protagonist
The arc closes with the flash-forward initial of the series: Boruto fights in a ruined Konoha
alternative_seeds: If Kawaki hadn't altered the memories, would a diplomatic solution have been possible?
If Sasuke had been present at the framing, would he have prevented Boruto's defection?
If Boruto had accepted his missing-nin status without seeking to prove his innocence, what future would that have created?

kurama_sealed_mito_uzumaki.name_en: Kurama seals into Mito Uzumaki by Hashirama
narrative_invariants: Kurama is sealed for the first time into a willing human
Mito Uzumaki becomes the first jinchuuriki of Kurama
The sealing represents an attempt to pacify the beast by Konoha
Kurama loses its freedom and develops a heightened hatred for humans
alternative_seeds: If Hashirama had sealed Kurama into himself rather than Mito, what impact would that have on the history of Konoha?
If Mito had refused the sealing, how would Konoha have controlled Kurama?
If Kurama had willingly accepted this first sealing, would its hatred for humans have been less?

kurama_transferred_kushina.name_en: Kurama transferred from dying Mito to Kushina Uzumaki
narrative_invariants: The transfer of a tailed beast is a rare and dangerous procedure
Kushina is chosen due to the unique chakra of the Uzumaki clan, capable of containing Kurama
This transfer inscribes Kurama in the destiny of the family that will give birth to Naruto
Mito passes on her knowledge of Kurama's management to Kushina
alternative_seeds: If no Uzumaki candidate had been available, how would Konoha have handled Mito's death?
If Kushina had refused the jinchuuriki role, would Kurama have been freed?
If Kushina had not had the special abilities of her clan, would the sealing have held?

kurama_controlled_obito_kushina_labor.name_en: Obito takes control of Kurama during Kushina's childbirth
narrative_invariants: The seal weakens during childbirth, offering Obito a unique opportunity
Kurama is used as a weapon against its own jinchuuriki and against Konoha
This event demonstrates the inherent vulnerability of the jinchuuriki system
Kurama is exploited without its consent, reinforcing its hatred for humans who manipulate it
alternative_seeds: If Obito had not known the seal's weakness during childbirth, would the attack on Konoha have occurred?
If Minato had neutralized Obito before Kurama's release, would Naruto's fate have changed?
If Kurama had resisted Obito's control even partially, would the damages have been less?

kurama_half_sealed_minato.name_en: Minato seals half of Kurama's chakra into himself before dying
narrative_invariants: Minato deliberately splits Kurama into two halves to weaken the beast and protect Naruto
The Yin half of Kurama is sealed with Minato in the Shinigami, depriving Naruto of part of his power for years
This division is a strategic measure that will have lasting consequences on Naruto's combat capabilities
Kurama is split, a unique situation among tailed beasts
alternative_seeds: If Minato had sealed Kurama entirely into Naruto, would the child have survived?
If the Yin half of Kurama had never been recovered, would Naruto have reached his full potential?
If Minato had chosen a different recipient than his own son, would the Kurama-jinchuuriki relationship have evolved differently?

kurama_naruto_alliance_bijuu_mode.name_en: Kurama voluntarily allies with Naruto and grants him the Bijuu mode
narrative_invariants: This is the first moment where Kurama freely and consciously chooses to cooperate with its jinchuuriki
Kushina's spiritual intervention plays a catalytic role in this reconciliation
This alliance marks a fundamental turning point in Kurama's arc, from a hateful monster to a willing protector
The Bijuu mode is only possible through true cooperation, not forced domination
alternative_seeds: If Naruto had not been confronted with Kurama's pure hatred at this moment, would the alliance have been possible?
If Kushina had not intervened spiritually, would Naruto have been able to convince Kurama alone?
If Kurama had definitively rejected the alliance, how would Naruto have faced the war?
If this alliance had been formed earlier, would Naruto's trajectory in his early years have changed?

suigetsu_captured_by_orochimaru.name_en: Suigetsu Captured and Imprisoned by Orochimaru
narrative_invariants: Orochimaru captures Suigetsu to experiment on him as on other rare shinobi
Suigetsu is kept alive in a partial dissolution state within a reservoir
This capture temporarily ends Suigetsu's quest for the blades
alternative_seeds: If Suigetsu had escaped Orochimaru, would he have gathered all the blades before Sasuke?
If Mangetsu was still alive, would Orochimaru have preferred to capture the older brother?
If Suigetsu had resisted, what level of power would he have reached without imprisonment?

suigetsu_liberee_rejoint_taka.name_en: Suigetsu Liberated by Sasuke and Joined Team Taka
narrative_invariants: Sasuke liberates Suigetsu in exchange for his loyalty and help to kill Itachi
Suigetsu recovers the Kubikiribocho, Zabuza Momochi's blade, as his first blade
Suigetsu's liberation marks the beginning of his conditional allegiance to Sasuke
alternative_seeds: If Suigetsu had refused to follow Sasuke, would he have alone recovered all the blades?
If Suigetsu had not recovered the Kubikiribocho, how would the Team Taka dynamic have changed?
If Suigetsu had chosen to return to Kirigakure instead of joining Taka, what role would he have played in the mist revolution?

suigetsu_vs_killer_b_hachibi.name_en: Suigetsu Fights Killer B and is Defeated with Team Taka
narrative_invariants: Killer B reveals himself to be an extraordinarily powerful jinchuuriki who crushes Team Taka
Suigetsu is incapacitated and temporarily loses the Kubikiribocho during the battle
Killer B simulates his capture, making Team Taka's mission a partial failure despite appearances
alternative_seeds: If Suigetsu had fully used his liquefaction technique, would he have lasted longer against Killer B?
If Team Taka had captured the real Killer B, how would Akatsuki have exploited the Hachibi?
If Jugo had not been present to help, would Suigetsu have survived the battle?

suigetsu_capture_karin_guerre_ninja.name_en: Suigetsu Captured by Alliance Shinobi and Imprisoned During the War
narrative_invariants: Suigetsu is captured separately from Sasuke after the temporary dissolution of Team Taka
During his captivity, Suigetsu discovers and reads important secret documents about Orochimaru's heritage
This period of isolation strengthens Suigetsu's independent character
alternative_seeds: If Suigetsu had not been captured, would he have joined Sasuke on the battlefield sooner?
If Suigetsu had not found Orochimaru's documents, would the revelation about Sarada have been possible?

suigetsu_teste_adn_revelation_sarada.name_en: Suigetsu Conducts an DNA Test That Reveals False Conclusions About Sarada's Lineage
narrative_invariants: Suigetsu compares the umbilical cord DNA of Karin with that of Sarada and incorrectly concludes Karin is the biological mother
This erroneous conclusion causes a major identity crisis for Sarada regarding her origins
Suigetsu's DNA test, though misinterpreted, is a crucial trigger for the Sasuke side story
alternative_seeds: If Suigetsu had conducted a more rigorous DNA analysis, would Sarada have avoided the identity crisis?
If Karin had immediately explained the origin of her umbilical cord, how would that have affected Sarada-Sakura's relationship?
If Suigetsu had kept the test results for himself, would Sarada still have sought Sasuke?

karin_warden_southern_hideout.name_en: Karin named guardian of Orochimaru's southern hideout
narrative_invariants: Karin is sufficiently reliable and powerful for Orochimaru to entrust her with managing a strategic hideout.
This position reveals the dual nature of Karin: a loyal servant but also a prisoner of fact within Orochimaru's system.
Karin possesses sensory perception and healing abilities that justify her value in Orochimaru's eyes.
alternative_seeds: If Karin had refused this position, would she have left Otogakure before Sasuke's arrival?
If Karin had revealed information about the hideout to other villages, what impact would it have on Orochimaru's arc?
If another person had been named guardian, would Sasuke have still recruited Karin into Taka?

karin_bitten_sasuke_sacrifice.name_en: Karin bitten by Sasuke during the battle against Danzo
narrative_invariants: Sasuke pierces Karin with his Chidori to reach Danzo, revealing his ability to sacrifice allies.
This act marks a turning point in Karin and Sasuke's relationship, breaking Karin's idealization.
Karin's survival is ensured by her capture by Konoha, distancing her definitively from Taka.
Sasuke's act against Karin illustrates his descent into darkness after Itachi's death.
alternative_seeds: If Karin had been killed by Sasuke, what impact would it have on the revelation of Sarada's identity?
If Karin had refused to help Sasuke against Danzo, would Taka have failed its mission?
If Karin had not been captured by Konoha, would she have joined Akatsuki or acted alone?

karin_captured_konoha_interrogation.name_en: Karin captured and interrogated by Ibiki Morino at Konoha
narrative_invariants: Karin's capture by Konoha places her in an ambiguous position between enemy and victim of Sasuke.
Karin holds information on Taka, Akatsuki, and Orochimaru's operations, making her a valuable asset.
Ibiki's interrogation highlights the brutality of the ninja world even toward injured prisoners.
alternative_seeds: If Karin had divulged all information on Sasuke, would his fate have been different?
If Konoha had freed Karin earlier, would she have played a role in the Fourth Ninja War?
If Karin had refused to talk, would Konoha have executed her as an enemy?

karin_uzumaki_clan_revelation.name_en: Revelation of Karin's affiliation with the Uzumaki clan and her connection with Kushina
narrative_invariants: Karin's Uzumaki heritage explains her exceptional regenerative and sensory abilities.
The dispersion of the Uzumaki clan after Uzushiogakure's destruction is illustrated by members like Karin found in other villages.
Karin shares a clan bond with Naruto and Kushina, adding a tragic dimension to her isolation.
alternative_seeds: If Karin had known her Uzumaki heritage earlier, would she have joined Konoha instead of Otogakure?
If Naruto and Karin had been aware of their clan bond, would it have changed their relationship?
If the Uzumaki clan had not been dispersed, would Karin have grown up in Uzushiogakure?

karin_sarada_naissance_cordon_ombilical.name_en: Karin assists in the birth of Sarada and cuts the umbilical cord
narrative_invariants: Karin is present at Sarada's birth and keeps the umbilical cord, causing later confusion about Sarada's biological mother.
Karin's preservation of the umbilical cord demonstrates her lingering feelings for Sasuke.
This event establishes an indirect but crucial link between Karin and Sarada's identity arc in Boruto.
Sakura is Sarada's biological and legal mother, the confusion resolved by DNA analysis.
alternative_seeds: If Karin had not kept the umbilical cord, would Sarada's identity crisis have occurred?
If Sasuke had been present at the birth, would Karin still have played the midwife role?
If Karin had immediately revealed why she kept the cord, would it have changed Sakura and Karin's relationship?

kinkaku_ginkaku_kyuubi_swallowed.name_en: Kinkaku and Ginkaku swallowed by the Kyuubi and survived for two weeks
narrative_invariants: Kinkaku and Ginkaku survived inside the Kyuubi by eating its flesh, absorbing part of its chakra
This event made Gold and Silver the only non-jinchuuriki humans to possess the chakra of the Nine-Tailed Fox, Ne
Their survival is considered a monstrous miracle that solidified their legend within Kumo
alternative_seeds: If Kinkaku and Ginkaku had not survived the Kyuubi, would Kumo have been able to develop its own bijuu-class weapons?
If the Kyuubi had killed the brothers, would the Five Celestial Treasures have been passed down differently?
If other ninjas had attempted the same survival inside the Kyuubi, would the nature of bijuu chakra be better understood?

kinkaku_ginkaku_kill_second_raikage.name_en: Kinkaku and Ginkaku kill the Second Raikage during a betrayal
narrative_invariants: Kinkaku and Ginkaku killed the Second Raikage, becoming the most infamous criminals in the history of Kumo
Despite this crime, Kumo chose to imprison them rather than execute them due to their exceptional power
This act of betrayal against their own Raikage defined their status as outlaws while keeping them as military assets too valuable to eliminate
alternative_seeds: If the Second Raikage had survived the betrayal by the brothers, what would Kumo's policy have been toward ninjas of this power?
If Kumo had executed the brothers after this murder, would the village have lost a major strategic advantage during the wars?
If the motives of the betrayal had been revealed publicly, would Kumo's history have changed?

kinkaku_emprisonnement_kumo.name_en: Kinkaku and Ginkaku imprisoned in the secret dungeons of Kumogakure
narrative_invariants: Kumo made the strategic choice to imprison the Gold and Silver Brothers rather than execute them, preserving their power for desperate situations
The prolonged imprisonment of the brothers reflects Kumo's moral dilemma facing criminals who are also war assets
Kinkaku remains a dormant asset of the village despite his crimes against his own Raikage
alternative_seeds: If Kumo had executed Kinkaku and Ginkaku, would the village have sought other ways to acquire the Kyuubi's chakra?
If the brothers had attempted to escape during their imprisonment, who would have been able to stop them?

kinkaku_liberation_quatrieme_guerre.name_en: Kinkaku released and deployed as Kumo's secret weapon during the Fourth Great Ninja War
narrative_invariants: Kinkaku is released from his prison by Kumo to serve as a last-resort weapon against Tobi's army during the Fourth Great Ninja War
His release reveals the true extent of his power and Kumo's desperate decision to use its most dangerous criminals
The activation of his Kyuubi-linked transformation represents an existential threat to the Shinobi Alliance on this battlefield front
alternative_seeds: If Kinkaku had refused to be used as a weapon by Kumo, how would the Alliance have held this front?
If the transformation of Kinkaku into the Kyuubi form had not been stopped by Darui, what would have been the losses for the Alliance?
If Kumo had released the brothers earlier in the war, would the outcome of certain battles have changed?

kinkaku_scelle_goudre_nuage_cratere.name_en: Kinkaku sealed in the Cloud Gourd and Crater by Darui
narrative_invariants: Kinkaku is sealed in the Cloud Gourd and Crater by Darui after entering berserk rage following the capture of Ginkaku
His uncontrollable rage caused by the loss of his brother is directly exploited to trap him, anger being his fatal weakness
The sealing of Kinkaku marks the end of the threat posed by the Gold and Silver Brothers on the first front of the Fourth Great Ninja War and cements Darui as an exceptional commander
alternative_seeds: If Kinkaku had not lost control after the capture of Ginkaku, would Darui have been able to seal him despite everything?
If the gourd had been broken after the sealing, would Kinkaku have been able to break free and resume his assault?
If Kinkaku had been sealed before Ginkaku, would the dynamics of the battle have been different for Darui?
If the power of the Kyuubi's chakra of Kinkaku had been used after his sealing as a source of energy, what would have been the effects on the gourd?

fugaku_becomes_uchiha_clan_head.name_en: Fugaku becomes head of the Uchiha clan and head of the Konoha military police
narrative_invariants: Fugaku holds the leadership of the Uchiha clan and the military police of Konoha, making him one of the most influential political figures in the village.
This dual authority places the Uchiha clan in an ambivalent position: guarantor of the village's order and a marginalized group after the Kyuubi attack.
Fugaku's legitimacy rests as much on his combat abilities as on his clan heritage.
alternative_seeds: If Fugaku had not become head of the military police, would the Uchiha clan have retained enough political weight to consider a coup d'état?
If a more conciliatory leader had directed the clan, would the rift with Konoha have been avoided?

fugaku_mikoto_marriage_itachi_birth.name_en: Marriage of Fugaku and Mikoto, birth of Itachi
narrative_invariants: Itachi is the first child of Fugaku and Mikoto, and carries the weight of the clan's expectations.
Fugaku invests heavily in Itachi's training, hoping to make him the symbol of the Uchiha clan's greatness.
The father-son relationship between Fugaku and Itachi is marked by mutual admiration tinged with distance and demand.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had not been so prodigious, would Fugaku have placed as much pressure on his elder son?
If Mikoto had had more influence on Fugaku's political decisions, would the clan have chosen a different path?

fugaku_plans_uchiha_coup.name_en: Fugaku orchestrates the Uchiha clan's coup d'état against Konoha
narrative_invariants: Fugaku is the main instigator of the coup d'état plan, motivated by the sense of injustice suffered by his clan after the Kyuubi attack.
By involving Itachi in the planning, Fugaku ignores or underestimates Itachi's loyalty to Konoha, sealing the clan's tragic fate.
The coup d'état plan is the direct consequence of the systematic marginalization of the Uchiha by Konoha's leaders, including Danzo and the old council.
alternative_seeds: If Fugaku had known that Itachi would transmit the coup plan to Konoha, would he have excluded his son or abandoned the plan?
If the Third Hokage had granted the Uchiha more rights after the Kyuubi attack, would the coup have been considered?
If Fugaku had succeeded in his coup, what form would Konoha have taken?

fugaku_reveals_sharingan_to_itachi.name_en: Fugaku reveals his Mangekyou Sharingan to Itachi and confides in him the secret of his power
narrative_invariants: Fugaku possesses the Mangekyou Sharingan, confirming he has experienced a traumatic loss in the past, though the details are not fully explained in canon.
By sharing this secret with Itachi, Fugaku expresses absolute trust and his vision of a future where the Uchiha clan dominates Konoha.
This revelation deepens Itachi's inner conflict, as he admires his father yet knows he must betray the village to save it.
alternative_seeds: If Itachi had accepted leading the coup with his father, would Konoha's history have been radically different?
If Fugaku had used his Mangekyou Sharingan during the massacre to defend himself, would Itachi have been able to defeat him?

fugaku_accepts_death_by_itachi.name_en: Fugaku and Mikoto accept their deaths at Itachi's hands during the clan massacre
narrative_invariants: Fugaku and Mikoto show no resistance to Itachi during their deaths, accepting their son's choice and expressing a form of pride and love in their final moments.
This acceptance turns the massacre into a family tragedy rather than a simple act of betrayal, and represents the heaviest emotional burden Itachi carries for life.
The death of Fugaku without resistance illustrates that, faced with the ultimate choice between the clan and his son, Fugaku implicitly chose Itachi.
alternative_seeds: If Fugaku had tried to fight Itachi during the massacre, would the outcome have been different or would Itachi's psychological burden have been deeper?
If Fugaku had survived the massacre, would he have forgiven Itachi or sought revenge?
If Sasuke had witnessed his parents' silent acceptance, would his hatred for Itachi have been as absolute?

kushina_kidnapped_kumo_jinchuriki.name_en: Kushina kidnapped by Kumo and rescued by Minato
narrative_invariants: Kushina chooses to leave strands of hair as a clue, establishing red hair as a symbol of her identity and determination
Minato is the only one to follow the clue and find Kushina, which starts their love story
The Uzumaki clan is targeted for their exceptional sealing abilities, justifying the kidnapping
alternative_seeds: If Minato hadn't followed the hair, would Kushina have been used as a jinchuriki by Kumo?
If Kushina hadn't left a clue, would the course of the Minato-Kushina relationship have existed?
If Konoha had intercepted the kidnapping officially, would the conflict with Kumo have erupted earlier?

kushina_devient_jinchuriki_kyuubi.name_en: Kushina becomes the second jinchuriki of Kurama
narrative_invariants: The transfer of the Kyuubi from one Uzumaki host to another confirms that the Uzumaki clan is specially suited to seal Kurama
Mito teaches Kushina the wisdom of turning hatred into love, establishing the central theme of the jinchuriki
Kushina came to Konoha precisely because she was destined to become a jinchuriki
alternative_seeds: If someone other than Kushina had been chosen, would the Uzumaki clan have maintained its link with Konoha?
If Kushina had refused this burden, who would have been the third jinchuriki of the Kyuubi?
If Mito had lived longer, would Kushina have had a more complete training?

kushina_accouchement_naruto_mort.name_en: Kushina gives birth to Naruto, is used as a catalyst for the Kyuubi, and dies
narrative_invariants: Kushina and Minato both die to protect Naruto and Konoha, passing on their love as their last message
Kushina uses her Uzumaki chains to keep Kurama in check so that Minato can complete the sealing
Kushina's death is directly linked to Obito's masked intervention, revealing Uchiha Madara's planning
Kushina uses her last strength to give life advice to newborn Naruto, establishing her maternal love as the foundation of Naruto's arc
alternative_seeds: If Kushina had survived, how would the mother-son relationship have influenced Naruto's development?
If Obito hadn't intercepted the birth, would Kurama have been sealed without an attack on Konoha?
If Minato had found another method, would Kushina's death have been avoided?

kushina_rencontre_naruto_dans_conscience.name_en: Kushina appears in Naruto's consciousness during the release of the Kyuubi
narrative_invariants: Kushina had planned for this moment by leaving a part of her chakra in Naruto's seal, proof of her foresighted love
The appearance of Kushina reveals that Naruto's parents had anticipated extreme crisis moments
This event precedes the complete revelation of Naruto's parents' identity and constitutes a first posthumous affective link
alternative_seeds: If Kushina hadn't left chakra in the seal, would Naruto have fully released Kurama against Pein?
If Naruto had immediately recognized Kushina, would that have changed his relationship with the parental loss?
If Kushina's chakra had been exhausted before this intervention, who would have stopped the Kyuubi's release?

kushina_raconte_histoire_naruto_dans_con.name_en: Kushina reveals her life and the story of the night of Naruto's birth
narrative_invariants: Kushina details her origins, her kidnapping, her jinchuriki status, and the night of the sealing, completing Naruto's narrative puzzle
This encounter is the only significant and prolonged interaction between mother and son, constituting the essence of their relationship
Kushina expresses regret for not being present for Naruto, humanizing the parental sacrifice beyond the simple hero
alternative_seeds: If Kushina hadn't been able to tell everything, would Naruto have forgiven Obito later?
If this encounter had happened earlier in Naruto's life, would his psychological development have been different?
If Naruto had been able to ask Kushina questions, what additional secrets would have been revealed?

tajima_uchiha_hashirama_senju_battlefiel.name_en: Tajima Uchiha confronts Butsuma Senju on the battlefield
narrative_invariants: Tajima embodies the generation of clan leaders who perpetuate war through brutal pragmatism
The confrontation between Tajima and Butsuma is the parental mirror of the duels between their sons Madara and Hashirama
The clan war is not a pure war of hatred but of survival and resources
alternative_seeds: If Tajima and Butsuma had negotiated a truce, would Madara and Hashirama have needed to meet in secret?
If Tajima had been killed by Butsuma, would Madara have become leader sooner and with even more rage?
If one of the two leaders had captured the other, what ransom or agreement could have arisen?

tajima_discovery_madara_hashirama_secret.name_en: Tajima discovers the secret meetings of Madara with Hashirama and plans an ambush
narrative_invariants: Tajima uses his own son as bait or instrument of war without conscience
This episode forces Madara to break openly with Hashirama at least temporarily, solidifying the tragic trajectory of the character
The fathers of the two future rivals are complicit in maintaining intergenerational hatred
alternative_seeds: If Madara had refused to obey Tajima and warned Hashirama, would clan alliances have arisen much earlier?
If the ambush had succeeded and killed Hashirama as a child, would Madara have sunk into madness earlier?
If Tajima had learned that Butsuma had done the same to Hashirama, would he have reconsidered his methods?

tajima_sons_killed_in_battle_senju.name_en: Several sons of Tajima are killed in battle against the Senju, with Izuna spared as a child
narrative_invariants: The deaths of Madara's brothers under Tajima's leadership etch loss as the identity foundation of the Uchiha clan
Tajima himself suffers the consequences of the war he perpetuates, no clan is spared
These griefs directly fuel Madara's sharingan and his future power
alternative_seeds: If no sons of Tajima had died, would Madara have developed his sharingan as powerfully?
If Tajima had sought peace after these losses rather than after, would the era of the villages have started a generation earlier?
If Izuna had died instead of another brother, would Madara's revenge arc have taken a different form?

tajima_uchiha_death_warring_states.name_en: Death of Tajima Uchiha during the Era of the Warring States
narrative_invariants: Tajima's death marks the transfer of leadership burden to Madara, cementing his destiny
Tajima embodies the sacrificed generation of perpetual war whose death is a logical but not heroic consequence
The passing of the torch between Tajima and Madara is a turning point between the era of warring clans and the era of negotiations leading to Konoha
alternative_seeds: If Tajima had lived long enough to see Konoha's foundation, would he have accepted the alliance with the Senju or sabotaged negotiations?
If Tajima had died before Madara was strong enough, would another Uchiha have taken leadership and changed the clan's history?
If Tajima had been killed by Butsuma in a duel, would Madara have sought personal revenge against Hashirama?
